repositories.brief_summary: Security fixes for CVE-2026-41083 (Filename.quote_command) and CVE-2026-28364 (intern.c) landed, with a GC mark_header regression fix.
repositories.new_features_summary: CVE fixes for Windows filename quoting and intern.c, runtime_events infinite loop prevention, and flambda stub inlining fix.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **shell injection vulnerability** in `Filename.quote_command` on Windows (CVE-2026-41083, OSEC-2026-05): filenames containing special characters like `&` in redirection arguments were not properly quoted, potentially allowing unintended command execution. [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) implemented the fix with report from Andrew Nesbitt and review by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#14853](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14853))
- Fixed **inverted check in `mark_header` for lazy tags**, a regression introduced in [#14816](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14816) that could cause corruption and segfaults in multi-domain programs using laziness. The fix was spotted in a regression sweep and verified by [Sadiq Jaffer](https://github.com/sadiqj) and [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) ([#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856)) by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- Fixed **infinite loop in `runtime_events.c`'s `write_to_ring`** when an event is larger than the configured ring buffer size. Previously, running the instrumented runtime with a small `OCAMLRUNPARAM=e=` value (e.g. `e=4`) during the first major GC in `caml_ev_alloc_flush` would hang forever. Now the runtime emits a descriptive fatal error and suggests the minimum required `e=` value (Michael Bacarella, review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- Fixed **infinite recursive inlining of stub functions with Flambda**, a miscompilation discovered by Nathanaëlle Courant and Vincent Laviron and fixed by Pierre Chambart ([#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828), [#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839))
- Fixed **modular explicit type expansion** to correctly update expansion nodes containing `tfunctor` arguments in `instance_tfunctor`, resolving an uncaught exception reported in [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842). Also restores type abbreviations involving user-written module-dependent types via `get_folded_desc` in `copy_sep` ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845))
- Updated **Changes file** to assign CVE numbers to the `intern.c` robustness fix (CVE-2026-28364, OSEC-2026-01 by Xavier Leroy and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)) and to fill in the previously placeholder entry for the **relocatable compiler** feature with full reviewer credits ([#13728](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13728), [#14014](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14014), [#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14243), [#14244](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14244), [#14245](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14245))
- Added **`AR` and `ARFLAGS` export in `Makefile.config`**, deprecating `ARCMD`, improving build system integration for external tooling ([Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), review by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and Samuel Hym, [#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- Updated stdlib documentation: corrected self-referential typo in `Option.is_some` docstring ([#14855](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14855)) and clarified `List.find_opt` documentation to refer to itself rather than `find` ([#14851](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14851)) by [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875)
- Corrected **`Patterns.deconstruct` documentation** to reflect that the function is total and does not raise exceptions, removing misleading language ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [#14859](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14859))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new Windows-related PRs opened alongside ongoing stdlib extension proposals for Weak, Dynarray, and extensible IO channels.
repositories.activity:
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened **more cross-compilation fixes for Windows** addressing dummy file extensions in `installcross`, CC variable quoting for Flexlink when flags contain spaces, and preventing clang-cl from synthesizing `wcslen` calls in `header.obj` by passing `-fno-builtin-wcslen` ([#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850))
- [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) opened a PR implementing **configurable domain stack size** via a new `S=` parameter in `OCAMLRUNPARAM`, addressing the long-standing feature request to allow spawning domains with larger stacks for C FFI-heavy workloads ([#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860), related to [#14195](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14195))
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a PR adding **missing memory model documentation comments** in `memory.c` explaining why atomic write functions end with a release fence and documenting "publication safety" for initialising writes into newly allocated values, drawing on discussions from [#10995](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10995) ([#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857))
- [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues work on **native `Iatomic_fetch_add` across all architectures** (amd64, arm64, RISC-V, POWER), with review from [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) focused on ensuring correct release fences required by the OCaml memory model beyond what C11 atomics guarantee ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) also has an open PR implementing **`Dynarray.sort`, `Dynarray.stable_sort`, and `Dynarray.stable_sort_sub`** by delegating to `Array.stable_sort_sub` on the backing array, with benchmarks showing the approach is faster than alternatives using `Dynarray.get`/`set` ([#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) continues seeking feedback on **`Weak.{iter, iteri, foldi_left}`** additions to the standard library, with `foldi_left` implemented as a C primitive to avoid per-element `option` wrapping allocations ([#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792))
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) maintains an open draft PR for **extensible IO channels** implementing the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md), currently under broader stdlib team discussion ([#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775))
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) has two open PRs: **`-rectypes` testing in expect tests** to exercise more typing behaviors in CI ([#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)) and **allowing aliases in signature inclusion checks** to permit type aliases to be followed when verifying module type inclusion ([#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open PR adding **weak pointer and ephemeron repro test cases** for the multicoretests failures from [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349), noting that tests now pass on trunk ([#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745))
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) also merged a fix for the **GHA cygwin-packages download directory** that was causing cache misses in CI (saving ~20 seconds per run) by correcting the path from `C:\cygwin-packages` to the actual default `D:` volume used by the action ([#14852](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14852))
- Long-standing **soundness bug with polymorphism and row types** ([#10840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10840), originally from 2021) was closed by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), noting that the reported crash examples are now rejected by the typechecker and all modular explicit examples are accepted
repositories.discussions_summary: Proposals for lighter deep record update syntax and newcomer onboarding questions were active this week.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Yotam Barnoy](https://github.com/bluddy) proposed **lighter syntax for deep record updates** in [#14854](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14854), suggesting `{bar with {foo with x=2}}` as an alternative to `{bar with foo={bar.foo with x=2}}`, with comparisons to the alternative `foo.x=2` dotted-path syntax from [#290](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/290)
- Two newcomers opened discussions seeking contribution guidance: [@gperdutoutmoncodetipe](https://github.com/gperdutoutmoncodetipe) in [#13901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13901) asking about approachable issues and [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) in [#14849](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14849) looking for project ideas — [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) subsequently contributed two documentation fixes ([#14851](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14851), [#14855](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14855)) that were merged this week
repositories.trends_summary: Windows compatibility and cross-compilation received sustained attention alongside security hardening with two CVE assignments.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows and cross-compilation work** is seeing sustained activity: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) landed the `Filename.quote_command` security fix ([#14853](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14853)), [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged a CI cache fix ([#14852](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14852)) and opened more `installcross` fixes ([#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850)), and a CI cleanup for Windows test processes also landed
- **Security hardening** received formal CVE assignments this week for two previously merged fixes: CVE-2026-28364 / OSEC-2026-01 for `intern.c` robustness and CVE-2026-41083 / OSEC-2026-05 for `Filename.quote_command` Windows injection
- **Newcomer contributions** are translating into merged work: [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) went from posting a newcomer Q&A discussion to having two documentation PRs merged within the same week, and [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) submitted PRs for both a domain stack size feature ([#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860)) and `Dynarray.sort` ([#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
- **Memory model documentation** is being actively improved: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)'s open PR ([#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857)) and the ongoing `Iatomic_fetch_add` review ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) both reflect careful attention to documenting and correctly implementing the OCaml multicore memory model guarantees
repositories.issues_summary: A long-standing documentation request to improve the C stub compilation guide remains open as a good first issue.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#5733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/5733) **Document using `ocamlc` as a driver for compiling C stublib sources** — the manual section on "Interfacing C with OCaml" is missing guidance on using `ocamlc` as a C compiler driver (automatic `-fPIC`, stdlib include path, `-ccopt`/`-cclib` flags, `ocamlc -config` for toolchain discovery). [@adithn875](https://github.com/adithn875) confirmed this is still undocumented in the 5.4 manual and expressed interest in tackling it after gaining more FFI experience

repositories.brief_summary: A CI test fix landed while several impactful PRs progressed: .install file extensions, API cleanup, and an opam init --bare bug fix.
repositories.activity_summary: Two new PRs opened targeting CI and init --bare behavior; ongoing work on .install extensions, API cleanup, tar library, and filename fixes.
repositories.activity:
- [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz) opened [#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957) to fix a performance issue where `opam init --bare` unnecessarily spent several seconds selecting a default compiler despite not needing one, by adding a `?no_compiler` parameter to `OpamClient` — fixes the long-standing [#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened [#6960](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6960) to fix two GitHub Actions depexts CI jobs: removing an unavailable `ocaml-compiler-libs` requirement from the Ubuntu job and forcing resolution for a conflict between `ocaml` and `ncurses-devel` on the openSUSE job
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) merged [#6961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6961) fixing a non-reproducible repository HTTP reftest where `opam admin index` embedded timestamps that caused failures on Windows CI when the minute changed mid-test ([46a13a4](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/commit/46a13a4bf2e60bd9c2f5c019166633d9660e859a))
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) continued work on [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938), adding `prefix` and `prefixexec` sections to `.install` files (would close [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919)), queued behind [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956); Ward inquired about the feature's readiness for the next release so that dune can begin depending on it
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) continued [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956) adding exhaustive reftests for all `.install` file fields; [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) provided detailed review feedback on log verbosity, test structure, and the embedded utility script organization
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) has [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) queued and marked ready-to-merge, adding a new `OpamTar` module using the `tar` OCaml library instead of the system `tar` tool for archive creation, in preparation for [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) continued [#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6953) fixing bugs in `OpamFilename.starts_with` and `OpamFilename.dir_starts_with` where `foo/bar` was incorrectly considered a prefix of `foo/bar-baz`, also adding a minimal unit test runner (fixes [#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948))
- [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio) continued [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) removing 366 unused exported values, fields, and constructors from the public API identified via dead code analysis, simplifying the `OpamHash` and `OpamSHA` APIs and deleting `src/state/shellscripts/prompt.sh`; CI feedback led to restoring a handful of values used by external projects (`opam-rt`, `spectrum_capabilities`, `opam-publish`, `opam-bundle`)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened issues [#6958](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6958) and [#6959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6959) tracking unclear error messages surfaced while working on [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956): the `misc` field emitting an unhelpful `expected abs-filename` and the `man` field emitting a bare `Not_found` when the filename lacks a section suffix
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [#6963](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6963) reporting that opam fails with exit code 99 when `git ls-files` is called internally in a repository owned by root but run as a different user, requesting graceful fallback behavior especially under `--yes`
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened [#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964) requesting the ability to demote `opam lint` errors to warnings via `--warnings`, specifically to allow opam-repository policy to reduce Error 46 to a warning (see [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998))
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) opened [#6965](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6965) reporting that `opam switch create 5.5.0~rc1` fails with a solver conflict on systems where a `relocatable` repository is set as default, while passing `--repo=default,relocatable` explicitly succeeds; the `--debug` output does not surface repository priorities, making diagnosis difficult
- [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) opened [#6962](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6962) reporting that `opam upgrade` across multiple switches caused `pa_ppx` to fail installation on OCaml < 5.3, suspecting a resolver regression
repositories.discussions_summary: A user questioned why opam's solver proposed downgrading ansifmt from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 as part of upgrading re.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) (lexa) opened [discussion #6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966) asking why `opam upgrade` proposed downgrading `ansifmt` from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 (a breaking change) in order to upgrade `re` to 1.14.0; this appears to be a case where the solver found a technically valid but semantically undesirable solution, raising questions about whether opam's default solver handles semantic versioning expectations appropriately
repositories.trends_summary: Active investment in .install file format improvements and ongoing API surface reduction through dead code analysis.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The `.install` file format is receiving a cluster of attention this week: [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) adds new `prefix`/`prefixexec` sections, [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956) adds exhaustive tests for all fields, and [#6958](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6958)/[#6959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6959) track error message improvements surfaced during testing
- The API reduction effort in [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) reflects growing attention to the library's public surface area, using automated dead code analysis tools and cross-referencing against known downstream consumers to identify safe removals
- CI test infrastructure continues to require maintenance, with [#6960](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6960) and [#6961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6961) both addressing flaky or broken CI jobs — the timestamp non-reproducibility in [#6961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6961) in particular illustrates the challenge of deterministic test output in tools that interact with time-sensitive metadata
repositories.issues_summary: Issue #6959 (better error for .install man field) is tagged 'easy first issue' and has a precise pointer to the relevant code.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#6959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6959) — **Better parsing error message for `.install` `man` field**: when the `man` field contains a filename without a section suffix (e.g., `man: [ "a-file" ]`), opam currently raises a bare `Not_found`. [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) has identified the root cause as an unguarded `String.index` call in [`src/format/opamFile.ml` lines 3789–3807](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/blob/cc3e1cc5c1de64e58fdd0eddfe347991a92eaefc/src/format/opamFile.ml#L3789-L3807), making it a well-scoped fix with a clear target
repositories.contributors_summary: R. Boujbel drove the week's merged work and new issue reports; Nathan Rebours, Corentin De Souza, Brian Ward, and others advanced ongoing PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [R. - [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz) opened [#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957) with a focused fix preventing unnecessary compiler selection during `opam init --bare`
- [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio) continued [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) and responded to CI failures that required restoring a handful of values used by external downstream libraries

repositories.brief_summary: Discussions on hover-on-warnings PR and an ocamlgrep integration proposal drove activity this week.
repositories.activity_summary: Two open PRs received updates and a new structural-search integration proposal generated maintainer discussion.
repositories.activity:
- **Hover on warnings** PR [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) (opened 2026-05-26, addressing issue [#717](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/717)) received review feedback this week, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) suggesting reuse of an existing compiler function for warning option parsing; [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) responded by identifying `Warnings.parse_options` from the OCaml compiler as the relevant upstream function.
- Automated **Nix flake lock update** PR [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) was updated on 2026-06-14, bumping the `merlin5_3` input (to 2026-06-13 snapshot) and `nixpkgs`/`nix-overlays` (to 2026-06-07 snapshots).
repositories.discussions_summary: A proposal to integrate ocamlgrep structural search into ocaml-lsp attracted responses from two maintainers.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened issue [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) proposing integration of **ocamlgrep** — a structural search tool that scans typed ASTs (`.cmt` files) — directly into ocaml-lsp, citing a VSCode prototype and a pre-draft integration PR under `mjambon#2`; the work is being done at LexiFi under [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)'s supervision.
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) responded that implementing the feature directly in **editor plugins** rather than in the LSP server makes more sense, given that there is little protocol functionality to piggyback on ([#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)).
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) noted that the revised VSCode integration prototype produces results by consuming JSON from an `ocamlgrep` command invocation, suggesting a clean separation between the tool and editor integration ([#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)).
repositories.trends_summary: Both open threads this week involve extending LSP hover and search capabilities using compiler-internal data.
repositories.emerging_trends: - Both active threads — **hover on warnings** ([#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606)) and **ocamlgrep structural search** ([#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) — involve surfacing richer information from typed compiler artifacts (warning metadata and typed ASTs) in the editor, reflecting a broader push to expose more of the OCaml compiler's analysis within IDE tooling.
repositories.contributors_summary: PixieDust, Ulysse, Martin Jambon, and Rudi Grinberg were active in PR reviews and issue discussions.
repositories.contributors:
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) is the author of the open **hover on warnings** PR [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606) and responded to review feedback by identifying the relevant compiler API (`Warnings.parse_options`) for warning parsing.
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed PR [#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606), suggesting the implementation reuse an existing compiler-side function for parsing warning options.
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened proposal issue [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) for integrating ocamlgrep into ocaml-lsp and provided follow-up context on the VSCode prototype approach.
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) commented on [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) advising that the feature is better suited to editor plugin implementations than to the LSP server itself.
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is mentioned in [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) as supervising the ocamlgrep integration work at LexiFi.

repositories.brief_summary: Fixed let-punning locate navigation, closed 4 stale issues, and opened a draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR.
repositories.new_features_summary: Fixed locate navigation for let-punning syntax; improved Emacs construct command nil-result handling.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **locate navigation in let-punning** syntax so that `locate` on a punned binding like `let x in` or `let+ x in` now correctly jumps to the source binding rather than reporting "Already at definition point" — resolves long-standing issues [#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796) and [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1579) via [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)
- Fixed **Emacs `merlin-construct` command** error handling by replacing `when-let` with `let`+`when` in `emacs/merlin.el`, avoiding incorrect behavior when the construct call returns nil ([bd869fb](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/bd869fb9e1d20999863f90de22e680d190587ef4))
repositories.activity_summary: Draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR opened; test suite decoupled from dune internal paths ahead of dune 3.24 changes.
repositories.activity:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) opened a **draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade** PR ([#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)) touching 462 files, representing the bulk of the work to keep Merlin in sync with the upstream compiler
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) decoupled the **no-dune test from dune internal install paths** by switching to `which ocamlmerlin` instead of hardcoded `install/default/bin/` paths, preventing breakage from the upcoming [ocaml/dune#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432) layout changes in dune 3.24; identified via reverse-dependency tests in [ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996) ([#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078))
- CI updated with **Emacs lint workflow** bumped to `purcell/setup-emacs@v8.0`, picking up Nix script updates and security fixes ([#2049](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2049))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) administratively **closed four stale issues**: [#1927](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1927) (signature help with type aliases), [#1533](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1533) (MenhirLib unbinding, closed as not planned), and triaged [#1610](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1610) and [#1748](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1748) with updated status notes
repositories.discussions_summary: Maintainer triaged open locate bugs for functor types and module import shadowing with updated reproduction notes.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed [#1610](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1610) (locate incorrectly jumps on types from functors) and noted that new location information in the typedtree for longidents may enable improvement, though the path is unclear
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) updated [#1748](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1748) (jump to incorrect module when `import.ml` and `Import` module coexist) with a concrete action plan: revive tests from [#1750](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1750), add the suggested regression test, and investigate a reasonable fix
- [@arvidj](https://github.com/arvidj) suggested in [#1921](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1921) that the `document` command could optionally return all doc comments from a module's signature, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noting this as a potential improvement path
repositories.trends_summary: Proactive upstream compatibility work (OCaml 5.5, dune 3.24) and resolution of let-punning edge cases in locate.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Proactive upstream compatibility**: both the OCaml 5.5 upgrade ([#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)) and the dune path fix ([#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078)) were driven by upcoming upstream changes rather than reactive breakage, showing a pattern of staying ahead of compiler and build-tool releases
- **Let-punning and punning syntax support**: the fix in [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066) closes two related issues ([#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796), [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1579)) about locate behavior in let-punning, reflecting continued work to support modern OCaml syntactic sugar in editor tooling
- **Issue triage and backlog hygiene**: multiple long-standing issues (dating to 2022–2025) were either closed or updated with current status, indicating an active effort to keep the issue tracker accurate
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors active: Paul-Elliot, Ulysse, Shon Feder, and Ali Caglayan across code, review, and issue triage.
repositories.contributors:
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) authored the **let-punning locate fix** ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)), including a detailed explanation of the folding-order root cause and test updates to make the Windows CI pass by omitting file paths from jq output
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed and merged [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066) and [#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078), opened the draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR ([#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)), triggered the dependabot rebase for [#2049](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2049), and triaged/commented on issues [#1533](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1533), [#1610](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1610), [#1748](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1748), and [#1921](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1921)
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) authored [#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078) to fix the no-dune test's dependency on dune internal paths, iterating from `dune describe location` to the simpler `which`-based approach after review feedback
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) diagnosed the impact of the dune 3.24 layout change on the test and suggested the `which`-based alternative approach in [#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078)

repositories.brief_summary: Heavy RPC and Memo refactoring week, with new Configurator APIs, package lock directory fixes, and a wave of documentation improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: New pkg-config variable query API in Configurator and expanded Memo module surface for introspection and parallel combinators.
repositories.new_features:
- **Configurator `query_variable`** adds `Pkg_config.query_variable` to query arbitrary pkg-config variables (e.g. `prefix`) per package by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#15078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15078)), closing a long-standing request ([#3332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3332))
- **Memo API expansion**: new `Node.Packed` existential type and `pack` constructor ([#15141](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15141)), `Spec` and `Table.spec` for frame downcasting ([#15143](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15143)), `node.mli` interface file ([#15124](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15124)), and `Deps.For_debugging.to_dyn` for structural dependency printing ([#15102](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15102)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Memo parallel combinators**: `all_concurrently_unit`, `parallel_iter_set`, and `Map.parallel_iter` added to round out the parallel surface ([#15149](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15149)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`Fiber.Pool.with_`**: new helper that creates a pool, runs a fiber alongside the pool runner, and closes it afterwards; used to simplify RPC session management ([#15207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15207)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`env_vars` alias** for environment variable stanzas introduced as a new feature ([#15187](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15187)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
repositories.activity_summary: The week was dominated by RPC and Memo module refactoring, package lock directory cleanup, and documentation fixes across dozens of merged PRs.
repositories.activity:
- **RPC server refactoring**: replaced the session object with a record ([#15211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15211)), removed `Rpc.Global` indirection ([#15202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15202)), moved RPC server creation to listen at socket creation time ([#15209](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15209)), and derived `dyn` values from repr for server state ([#15208](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15208)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Build loop API overhaul**: `Build_loop` was redesigned with `submit_rpc_request`, `cancel_rpc_requests_by_session`, and a simplified `poll` interface with a `sticky_goal` parameter, enabling independent completion of concurrent RPC builds ([#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Package lock directory nil argument fix**: arguments with always-false filters were left as `(when false "")` noise in generated lock directories; now dropped at assembly time ([#15128](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15128)) by [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7), following Slang simplification fix ([#15092](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15092))
- **pkgconf `--personality` version guard**: `dune-configurator` now only passes `--personality <target>` to pkgconf when it is version 1.7.0 or later, fixing breakage on RHEL 8 systems ([#15142](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15142)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)
- **mdx corrections as anonymous actions**: mdx correction generation is now attached directly to the runtest alias, removing the standalone `.mdx/*.corrected` rule ([#14807](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14807)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`dune init` module name validation**: project module names are now validated early to catch invalid names before the build ([#15014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15014)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Memo internal refactoring**: renamed `Cell` to `Node`, `Make_parallel_map` to `Map`, moved invalidation into its own module ([#15135](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15135), [#15139](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15139), [#15140](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15140)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`command_alias` helper moved** from `bin/util` to `bin/common` for wider reuse ([#15205](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15205)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`dune describe pkg lock` autolocking test** added to cover the autolocking workflow ([#15066](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15066)) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)
- **Documentation**: clarified `dune exec` argument separator usage ([#15131](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15131)), what `@doc` generates ([#15129](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15129)), generated target permissions ([#15126](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15126)), OCaml flag defaults linkage ([#15127](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15127)), executable linking modes ([#15184](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15184)), alias interaction with tests ([#15185](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15185)), build profile semantics ([#15051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15051)), and alias-only rules ([#15069](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15069)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
repositories.discussions_summary: User questions this week covered documentation gaps around foreign archives, virtual libraries, and `(allow_empty)` in dune-project files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- A user visiting Inria collected feedback on documentation shortcomings, opening a broad documentation improvement request ([#15153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15153)) — many of the linked issues were closed this week as documentation PRs landed
- Questions arose around **foreign archive static vs dynamic linking behavior** and whether **virtual libraries** can be used without building executables ([#15150](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15150), [#15151](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15151))
- A new user encountered a confusing `(allow_empty)` error in dune-project that was not documented ([#15152](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15152))
repositories.trends_summary: The week reflects a sustained effort to harden the RPC watch-mode build path and expand the Memo module's public API for testing and introspection.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **RPC architecture hardening**: the build loop API redesign (separating RPC-triggered builds from file-watch-triggered rebuilds) and the `Fiber.Pool.with_` helper point toward more robust concurrent watch-mode handling ([#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191), [#15207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15207))
- **Absolute path handling audit**: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened a batch of sub-issues under [#12230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12230) tracking commands that crash or misbehave on absolute paths (`dune utop`, `dune ocaml top`, `dune show targets`, `dune fmt` over RPC, etc.), each with a companion test PR
- **Memo surface expansion for tooling**: the week's Memo feature PRs (`Node.Packed`, `Spec`, `Deps.For_debugging`, parallel combinators) suggest preparation for richer external tooling or introspection use cases
- **Package management quality**: fixes to nil run arguments in lock directories ([#15128](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15128)) and symlink caching ([#14509](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14509)) reduce noise and unnecessary re-fetches in package workflows
repositories.issues_summary: The only active good-first issue is support for formatting individual files with `dune fmt`.
repositories.good_first_issues: - **`dune fmt` single-file formatting**: extend `dune fmt` to accept individual file paths on the command line rather than always formatting the whole project ([#7287](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7287))
repositories.contributors_summary: Rudi Grinberg drove the vast majority of activity; Ali Caglayan, Chizy, David Allsopp, and Sudha Parimala also contributed merged work.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) led the week with extensive RPC refactoring, Memo API expansion, documentation fixes, and test infrastructure work across dozens of PRs
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened the absolute-path handling audit ([#12230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12230)) and contributed test PRs for each affected command, plus a 3.24.0 backport ([#15091](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15091))
- [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7) fixed nil argument leakage in package lock directories ([#15128](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15128)) and the Slang simplification issue ([#15092](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15092))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) fixed the pkgconf `--personality` version guard for RHEL 8 compatibility ([#15142](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15142))
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) added a test case for `dune describe pkg lock` with autolocking ([#15066](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15066))

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml modalities rendering landed, zero_alloc arity display improved, and ppx_expect dependency removed from tests.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml modalities now rendered in all output formats; zero_alloc arity omitted when it matches function arity.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml modalities** are now rendered across all output backends (HTML, LaTeX, man pages, markdown), showing annotations like `@@ portable contended` on record fields, value bindings, and constructor arguments — resolves [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416) via [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- **`zero_alloc` arity annotation** is now omitted when it matches the arity of the annotated function, reducing visual noise in generated docs and making non-default arities stand out more clearly ([#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
repositories.activity_summary: One PR opened for internal Component.Fmt improvements; test infrastructure migrated away from ppx_expect and odoc-bench removed.
repositories.activity:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) opened [#1448](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1448) to improve `Component.Fmt` formatting, described as groundwork for upcoming documentation of odoc internals — not yet merged
- The `ppx_expect` dependency was **removed from the test suite** and replaced with plain diff-rule snapshot testing, with parser and semantic tests reorganised into `.expected` files and fully-qualified test executable names; this reduces the overall dependency footprint ([#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), reviewed by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)
- The **`odoc-bench.opam` file was removed** as the benchmarking setup had not been running for some time and was pulling unnecessary dependencies into normal test runs ([#1447](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1447)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- A **CI lower-bound fix** was merged bumping the minimum `ocamlfind` version to `>= 1.9.8` in `with-test` context, resolving a build failure on older versions that had been blocking RISC-V CI jobs ([#1446](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1446)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
repositories.discussions_summary: OxCaml modality vs mode distinction clarified in PR review; polymorphic variant inherit doc-comment blocker traced to OCaml compiler.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) review, [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) clarified the distinction between OxCaml *modalities* and *modes* for specific value patterns, with a follow-up modes PR planned
- [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) provided a detailed investigation of [#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846) (polymorphic variant inherit doc-comment rendering), tracing the issue to the OCaml compiler's typed tree — upstream compiler work in [ocaml/ocaml#1903](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/1903) would be needed before odoc can fix this
- RISC-V CI timeouts were discussed in [#1446](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1446) and [#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445); the solution involved both the ocamlfind lower-bound fix and reducing the dependency count by removing ppx_expect, with CI admins raising the timeout
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on OxCaml feature support and reducing test/build dependencies for a leaner CI.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **OxCaml support initiative** tracked in [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) is progressing steadily, with modalities ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and zero_alloc arity ([#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)) landing this week, and `include functor` support ([#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419)) still open
- **Dependency reduction** is a recurring theme: `ppx_expect` removed ([#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)) and `odoc-bench.opam` dropped ([#1447](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1447)), both aimed at making the build and CI faster and simpler
repositories.issues_summary: One good first issue open: polymorphic variant inherit doc-comment rendering, with upstream compiler investigation needed.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846) — **Comments on polymorphic variant `inherit` are not rendered** (opened by [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow)): doc-comments on inherited variant types (e.g. `| bar (** bar *)`) are silently dropped. Recent investigation by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) identified that the OCaml compiler's typed tree does not carry the attribute for `Rinherit`, so a fix in the compiler's parser is a prerequisite. Good entry point for contributors interested in the OCaml compiler/odoc interface
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors active: Jon Ludlam merged tests and cleanup PRs, Marek Kubica fixed CI and OxCaml rendering, art-w landed modalities support.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) merged the ppx_expect removal ([#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)) and odoc-bench cleanup ([#1447](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1447)), opened the Component.Fmt improvement ([#1448](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1448)), and reviewed the modalities PR ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420))
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) merged the zero_alloc arity omission ([#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)) and CI ocamlfind lower-bound fix ([#1446](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1446))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored the OxCaml modalities support PR ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), incorporating review feedback from [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) reviewed both [#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444) and [#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445), providing detailed feedback on test organisation and code structure

repositories.brief_summary: Workshop 2026 UI contrast fix, new CLAUDE.md developer guide, wasm_of_ocaml WASI backstage post, and several pending content PRs.
repositories.new_features_summary: White-text fix on Workshop date pills, CLAUDE.md added, HACKING.md expanded, and backstage post on wasm_of_ocaml WASI prototype.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **contrast on OCaml Workshop 2026 date pills** by switching from near-black `text-title` to `text-white` on the orange background badges for the submission deadline (1 Jul), speaker notification (27 Jul), and workshop (24 Aug) dates across both `/ocaml-workshop-2026` and `/ocaml-workshop-2026/cfp` pages ([#3673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3673))
- Added a new **`CLAUDE.md` developer guide** providing AI-assisted development context for the ocaml.org codebase, covering build commands, architecture overview, the EML templating system, data flow, dependency pinning, environment variables, CI workflows, and deployment pipeline ([#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))
- Expanded **`HACKING.md`** with a new "Before Submitting a PR" section covering `make fmt` and `markdownlint-cli2` requirements, an updated `tool/` directory tree listing all five tools (`data-packer`, `data-scrape`, `static-file-digest`, `tailwind`, `voodoo_serialize`), added `ocamlorg_static` to the `src/` listing, and corrected the dependency pinning file list from a vague "three places" to all five explicit files ([#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))
- Published new **Backstage OCaml article on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI prototype** describing the working WASI backend for `wasm_of_ocaml` targeting WASI preview1, with example commands for Wizard engine and Wasmtime, details on CPS effects compilation, and a call for a production user or sponsor to carry it to release ([#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))
- Added seven new **OCaml Planet blog posts** through automated scraping, including posts from jonludlam, tunbury, chrisarmstrong, cwn (OCaml Weekly News), and anil across three merged scrape PRs ([#3674](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3674), [#3676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3676), [#3678](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3678))

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 landed alongside a wave of new packages spanning AWS SDKs, database tooling, and networking libraries.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages added this week include AWS client libraries, database migration tooling, unikernel networking, and developer environment tooling.
repositories.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.5.0~rc1** added to the repository via [#30023](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30023), packaging `ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, and `ocaml-variants...+options` with runtime fixes, a Windows filename quoting security fix, and documentation updates.
- **awskit** (10 packages, 0.1.0) — AWS infrastructure library with signing, credentials, S3 client, and Eio/Lwt runtime adapters, added via [#29980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29980).
- **migra** (1.0.0 and 1.0.1) — database migration tool supporting PostgreSQL, MariaDB/MySQL, and SQLite with checksum validation and dry-run support, added via [#30002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30002) and [#30012](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30012); migra.2.0.0 followed via [#30033](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30033).
- **mnet** (6 packages, 0.0.3) — TCP implementation for Miou and Solo5 unikernels, now including a new `mnet-ssh` package for SSH in unikernels, added via [#30011](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30011).
- **cabal** (0.1.0) — Caml Agent Backend Abstraction Library providing backend-agnostic agent execution primitives for OCaml host applications driving agentic CLI backends, added via [#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008).
- **ocaml-ai-sdk** (2 packages, 0.4) — provider abstraction for AI models with extended Anthropic and OpenRouter prompt caching support, added via [#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005).
- **transom** (0.2.0) — OCaml backend for desktop WebView apps with typed IPC and generated glue code, added via [#30037](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30037).
- **contract** (0.2.0) — typed HTTP API contract library for OCaml with OpenAPI 3.0.3 output, added via [#30014](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30014).
- **doctor** (0.4.0) — diagnostic CLI for OCaml development environments checking opam, dune, LSP, and editor integration, added via [#30015](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30015).
- **luna** (0.1.0) — new package added via [#30030](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30030).
- **msts** (0.1.0) — new package added via [#30045](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30045).
- **liquid-ml** (5 packages, 0.1.4) — Shopify Liquid templating language implementation in OCaml with whitespace control and improved parser, added via [#30004](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30004).
- **melange-edn** (0.1.0 and 0.3.0) — EDN reader and writer for Melange targeting JavaScript, added via [#29999](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29999) and [#30028](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30028).
- **kind2** (3.0.0) — SMT-based automatic model checker for Lustre programs, added via [#30019](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30019).
- **notty-miou** (4 packages, 0.0.2) — terminal library using Miou with new support for mkernel/mnet, added via [#30027](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30027).
- **mcrunch** (0.0.3) — file crunching tool that now handles empty files, added via [#30025](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30025).
- **checked_oint** (2.0.1) — OCaml library for checked integer arithmetic, added via [#30031](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30031).
- **miou** (0.7.0) — composable concurrency primitives adding `Miou.get` and `Miou.Promise.state`, added via [#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009).
- **caqti** and **caqti-lwt** (2.3.2) — database access library fixing timed cleanup of idle connection pool entries, added via [#30026](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30026).
- **timedesc** and related (6 packages, 3.1.2) — OCaml datetime handling library suite updated via [#29995](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29995).
- **Frama-C** (32.1-Germanium) — static analysis platform for C, added via [#30016](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30016).
- **goblint-cil** (2.1.0) — C frontend for program analysis with cast kinds, `_Static_assert` checking, and attribute handling improvements, added via [#30022](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30022).
- **hxd** (0.5.0) — hexdump library with new introspection API for `cfg` values, added via [#30010](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30010).
- **ca-certs-nss** (3.125) — X.509 trust anchors updated to NSS 3.125 (2026-06-11), added via [#30038](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30038).
- **dockerfile** (3 packages, 8.4.1) — Dockerfile eDSL for OCaml with a libzstd packaging fix, added via [#30018](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30018).
- **embedded_ocaml_templates** (0.8.1) — OCaml template library making `ppx_inline_test` a test-only dependency, added via [#29929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29929).
- **duras** (2.0.0) — updated via [#29997](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29997).
- **ez_file** (0.4.0) — file manipulation library from OCamlPro, added via [#30006](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30006).
- **mingw-w64-shims** (1.0.1) — Windows shims package updated with clarified conf- usage, merged via [#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30040).
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw extensive package additions alongside infrastructure improvements for Windows CI and dune 3.24 compatibility fixes.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened, not yet merged**: [#30041](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30041) adds missing constraints to `async_websocket.v0.13.0` to prevent OxCaml switches from selecting the package; [#30042](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30042) drops a duplicate dune version constraint in `ez_file`; [#30043](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30043) submits `syguslib-utils.0.3.0` (SyGuS Language parser); [#30024](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30024) releases `store.0.2`, `store-dict.0.1`, `store-pushqueue.0.1` supporting user-defined storable types; [#30013](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30013) submits `diffast.0.4` (AST differencing), stalled on Cygwin linker errors and a RISC-V segfault; [#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30007) releases `ocaml-r.0.7.0` (OCaml bindings for the R interpreter), pending discussion on macOS failures; [#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29883) submits `lstar-rocq.1.0` (formally-verified L* in Rocq).
- **Windows CI hardened**: [#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998) updated Windows CI to install edited packages by upgrading from the master version first, catching state regressions and lockfile issues.
- **conf- packages updated** for 0install solver compatibility on Windows via [#30029](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30029), tweaking many `conf-*` packages and adding `conf-pkg-config.5`.
- **dune 3.24 compatibility**: Tests for `crunch.4.0.0` [#30035](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30035), `lintcstubs.0.4.7` [#30032](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30032), and `merlin` [#30044](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30044) were conditionally gated on `dune < 3.24` due to install layout changes.

- **diffast-core** 0.2 incompatibility with 0.4 noted and patched via [#30039](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30039).
- **CI**: A lint error 46 suppression added in [#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998) to avoid blocking CI when `conf`-flagged packages have install sections.

repositories.brief_summary: WASI 0.1 runtime landed, docs migrated to odoc/wodoc, and a systematic JS runtime review produced 13 merged bug fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: WASI 0.1 support via --enable wasi flag and full documentation migration to odoc + wodoc.
repositories.new_features:
- **WASI 0.1 runtime** support added to `wasm_of_ocaml` via `--enable wasi` flag ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)). Produces Wasm output runnable directly by Wizard engine (`wizeng.x86-64-linux -ext:stack-switching -ext:legacy-eh`) and wasmtime (`wasmtime -W=all-proposals=y`). Includes WASI support for separate compilation, exception handling instructions, and on-demand libc.wasm recompilation without Docker for normal builds.
- **Documentation migrated** from wiki format (`*.wiki`) to odoc + wodoc (`*.mld`), unified under `dune build @doc` ([#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)). The manual and API docs now share the Ocsigen site chrome via wodoc theming, are published to gh-pages at `https://ocsigen.org/js_of_ocaml/`, and render on ocaml.org without a fork. The per-project doc build script, Python tooling, and highlight.js are removed; internal link correctness is verified by odoc at build time.
- Fixed **`caml_dynlink_open_lib`** to drop the `mode` argument on OCaml >= 5.1, where the removed parameter caused `TypeError`; now returns the correct library slot index ([#2294](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2294)).
- Fixed **`Ephemeron.blit_key`** which was overwriting the destination's data with the source's data, corrupting ephemeron state ([#2264](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2264)).
- Fixed **Str engine SIMPLEOPT/SIMPLESTAR/SIMPLEPLUS** opcodes to bound-check the string position; a negated character class at end-of-string could loop forever ([#2277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2277)).
- Fixed **unmarshalling of BLOCK32** to use an unsigned shift for size decoding; blocks with 2^21 or more fields were silently read as single-field blocks, corrupting the stream ([#2275](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2275)).
- Fixed **unmarshalling of big-endian double arrays** (`CODE_DOUBLE_ARRAY32_BIG`) to register in the object table; shared back-references after such an array resolved to the wrong object ([#2276](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2276)).
- Fixed **`Int64.shift_right`** sign extension for negative values with shift counts 41-47; the sign bits did not reach the low limb, causing wrong results ([#2278](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2278)).
- Fixed **`Printf` `#` alternate flag** to not add a base prefix to zero; `"%#x" 0` printed `0x0` where native prints `0` ([#2266](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2266)).
- Fixed **`Open_append`** handling on the fake filesystem to seek to end-of-file on open; writes were incorrectly overwriting from offset 0 ([#2265](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2265)).
- Fixed **`Open_append`** to imply write access as in the C runtime; `open_out_gen [Open_append; Open_creat]` without `Open_wronly` previously produced a channel that failed on write ([#2267](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2267)).
- Fixed **`Sys.rename`** to the same path on the fake filesystem; renaming a file to itself previously deleted it ([#2268](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2268)).
- Fixed **`flush`** on a closed `out_channel` to do nothing rather than raise `Sys_error`; the OCaml manual specifies this as a no-op ([#2269](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2269)).
repositories.activity_summary: A wave of 23+ newly opened runtime correctness PRs from a systematic review complements 13 merges this week.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened runtime fix PRs** from [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)'s systematic JS runtime review ([#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270)) include: **exact float formatting, hex float parsing, and ldexp single-rounding** ([#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280)); **float array hashing and abstract block skipping** ([#2283](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2283)); **filesystem fixes** across fake device, node, QuickJS, and fs.js ([#2286](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2286), draft); **Unix fixes** for error_message, localtime, chmod, close, and readdir ([#2287](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2287), draft); **fresh channel per open_descriptor** call to fix shared channel state ([#2288](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2288)); **Sys.command exit code, Sys.getenv, Sys.isatty** ([#2289](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2289)); **compare ordering of immediate vs custom block** ([#2290](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2290)); **Domain.spawn exception handling** for JS and Wasm ([#2291](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2291)); **float array marshalling** as CODE_DOUBLE_ARRAY ([#2292](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2292)); **floatarray op result tags and Array.make error message** ([#2293](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2293)); **Graphics plot/arc/draw_image** origin and angle fixes ([#2296](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2296)); **QuickJS fd read/write error handling** ([#2297](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2297)); **bigarray hash tail zero-extension** for JS and Wasm ([#2271](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2271)); and **Weak.get_copy / Ephemeron.get_data_copy** byte and float array copying ([#2281](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2281)).
- **Wasm runtime cleanup** PR ([#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262)) opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) (draft), using `$wasi` preprocessor variables, removing Binaryen tuples and pop instructions, fixing types, and adding missing function names so that the `wax` tool can parse all runtime files.
- The long-running **WASI runtime PR** ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged after an extensive review covering the WASI preview1 implementation, test coverage with OCaml test suite, Wizard/wasmtime engine support, and separate compilation.
- **Documentation migration** ([#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)) merged by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat), replacing the wiki pipeline with odoc/wodoc and retiring the Python script, highlight.js, and template HTML. [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reviewed and identified several `wodoc convert` artifacts including bare URL mangling, nested list flattening, inline code brace issues, and dead cross-package `{!…}` links.
- **Release tracking** for js_of_ocaml 6.4 continues in [#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241), with OCaml 5.5.0 release expected imminently.
repositories.discussions_summary: Two comprehensive AI-assisted runtime reviews produced detailed bug tracking issues for both the JS and Wasm runtimes.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed [#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270) documenting a systematic review of all files in `runtime/js/*.js`, with differential testing against BigInt oracles (200k/20k cases), RFC test vectors for MD5/BLAKE2, 300-case zstd differential, and fuzzed mlBytes/UTF-8 codec tests. The review catalogues uncatchable crashes, wrong results, and parity divergences across 30+ issues, many of which became PRs this week.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed [#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263) with results of a parallel review of `runtime/wasm/*.wat` files, covering traps-instead-of-exceptions, wrong results in hash/domain/bigarray/io/stdlib modules, and parity divergences between the JS and Wasm runtimes. Several items (float array hashing, domain spawn exception handling) directly cross-reference JS fixes being submitted as PRs.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed [#2282](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2282) identifying two holes in `js_output.ml`'s `contains ~in_` traversal where a top-level `in` operator can escape the for-init parenthesization check in ECond branches and EArrow concise bodies, producing syntactically invalid output when re-printing parsed JavaScript.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed [#2285](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2285) identifying unsound corner cases in `js_simpl.ml` and `js_traverse.ml`: an if-fusion that can collapse two evaluations of an effectful expression into one in release builds, constant-if folding that drops `var` declarations from dead branches (a `ReferenceError` in strict/ESM contexts), and `free` not traversing catch parameters.
- In [#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) provided detailed review of the wiki-to-mld conversion, noting systematic `wodoc convert` artifacts (bare URL mangling, nested list flattening, inline code brace issues, silent dead cross-package links), and documented them as patterns worth checking in other Ocsigen project migrations.
repositories.trends_summary: AI-assisted systematic runtime review is driving a concentrated wave of correctness fixes across both JS and Wasm runtimes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Systematic AI-assisted correctness reviews** are surfacing clusters of related bugs across the runtime rather than individual one-off reports. Both [#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263) and [#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270) use a review-plus-differential-testing methodology (BigInt oracles, RFC vectors, fuzz runs) to verify findings, with the same approach being extended to the compiler output (`js_output.ml`, `js_simpl.ml`).
- **JS/Wasm runtime convergence** is an active theme: many bug fixes explicitly cross-reference divergences between `runtime/js/*.js` and `runtime/wasm/*.wat`, and the Wasm runtime cleanup PR [#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262) aims to make the Wasm files parseable by the `wax` tool, enabling the same review methodology to be applied more systematically.
- **Test-first commit structure** is used consistently in all the runtime fix PRs: buggy output is recorded as an expect test expectation first, then the fix flips the expectation to native-verified output, providing a regression baseline and demonstrating the bug clearly.
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors were active: hhugo with extensive runtime review and fixes, Jérôme Vouillon with WASI and Wasm cleanup work, and Vincent Balat with documentation.
repositories.contributors:

Merged 11 runtime fix PRs this week ([#2264](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2264), [#2265](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2265), [#2266](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2266), [#2267](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2267), [#2268](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2268), [#2269](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2269), [#2275](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2275), [#2276](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2276), [#2277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2277), [#2278](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2278), [#2294](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2294)), and opened an additional 15+ follow-up fix PRs from the same reviews. Also provided thorough review of the documentation migration PR [#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261), identifying conversion artifacts and verifying the assembled wodoc output.
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) landed the WASI runtime PR [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) after an extended review period, and opened the Wasm runtime cleanup draft PR [#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262) to standardize preprocessor variable naming and remove non-standard Binaryen extensions.
