repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.4 upstream merged to main, accompanied by post-merge fixes, Merlin sync work, and a wave of new PRs across types, runtime, and backend.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.4 upstream merge lands; runtime fix for lazy header atomicity; OCAMLRUNPARAM graceful handling; magic number bumped.
repositories.new_features:
- The **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** ([#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) landed on June 12, bringing in the full 5.4 branch from upstream OCaml. This includes stdlib updates, runtime changes, toolchain improvements, and a companion **magic number bump** to signal the new binary format.
- **Correct handling of concurrent lazy header marking** in the major GC: a bug introduced in [#6109](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6109) could cause SEGVs or heap corruption when two domains raced on a lazy block (one marking, one short-circuiting). Backported from [ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856) ([#6228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6228)).
- **Graceful degradation for `OCAMLRUNPARAM=d=`** values that are too high: the runtime now avoids a fatal error when the debug verbosity value exceeds valid range ([#6215](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6215)).
- **Post-5.4-merge typing fixes** by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin): corrected `@untagged` error messages on externals involving `int8`/`int16`, updated format strings in `typetexp.ml`, and unified duplicate `solve_Pexp_field` / `type_label_projection` functions from the upstream merge ([#6247](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6247)).
- **CFG merge-blocks register equality strengthened**: registers were compared using physical equality rather than structural equality in the merge-blocks pass, potentially missing legal merges ([#6223](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6223)).
- **Merlin updated to 5.2.0minus-40** with a merlin-specific diff added to `out_type.ml` ([#6211](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6211), [#6216](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6216), [#6225](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6225)).
repositories.activity_summary: Post-5.4 merge activity dominates, with 40 new PRs opened spanning types, runtime, name mangling tests, and backend infrastructure.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened: mode error reporting improvements** from [ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) with an annotation-hint leaf that prints the source location of the annotation explaining a mode constraint ([#6249](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6249)), and a draft PR reworking subsumption mode error reporting to print the mode error directly rather than mismatching signatures ([#6227](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6227)).
- **Newly opened: unboxed types representation work** from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) with PRs requiring `[@all_void_constructor]` on constructors with all-void arguments ([#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256)), fixing inline record representation for `any` fields ([#6240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6240)), and a draft restricting variant manipulation to types where all constructors have determined layouts ([#6238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6238)).
- **Newly opened: runtime API addition** — [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a draft PR introducing `caml_add_extent` to the runtime, allowing external memory regions of valid OCaml blocks to be handed to the GC ([#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)), and separately a PR to retire runtime 4 and remove the dual-runtime technical debt across 2,000+ files ([#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)); also opened [#6252](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6252) fixing ZMM28 save/restore.
- **Newly opened: compiler flag additions** — [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) opened draft PRs adding `-nondep <module>` to erase a module from generated `.cmi` ([#6229](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6229)) and forcing dynamic staticity for modules without a guaranteed `.cmx` via `-Ix` ([#6226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6226)).
- **Newly opened: name mangling test infrastructure** from [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) with four PRs split from [#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552): dedicated mangling tests for both schemes via per-unit override ([#6234](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6234)), zero-alloc checker test updates ([#6235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6235)), llvmize test coverage for both schemes ([#6236](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6236)), and ocamltest predicate additions for flat vs. structured mangling ([#6237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6237)); [shym](https://github.com/shym) also opened improvements to structured mangling for nested anonymous functions and modules ([#6242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6242)).
- **Newly opened: Merlin infrastructure** from [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923): a CI script `assert-in-sync.sh` to enforce Merlin stays in sync with frontend changes ([#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)), enabling ikinds by default in Merlin ([#6241](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6241)), and a Merlin upgrade for 5.4.0+ox1 ([#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)).
- **Newly opened: stdlib and typing** — [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) opened a PR to change most stdlib/built-in parameterised types to accept `any` ([#6253](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6253)) and fix a crash in `transl_idx` when encountering an `any` field ([#6246](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6246)); [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) opened PRs for binding op modes ([#6254](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6254)) and recording unyielding calls in bytecode debug events ([#6251](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6251)).
- **Newly opened: small numbers and effects** — [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) opened a PR to add small number types as hidden types in `env` (enabling upstream-compatible shimming) ([#6232](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6232)); [dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) opened a PR forcing effect pattern-matching to legacy mode to prevent potential unsoundness ([#6230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6230)) and a draft importing modular explicits from upstream ([#6243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6243)).
- **Newly opened: fexpr and Flambda 2** — [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) opened PRs adding method call support to fexpr ([#6222](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6222)) and filling in miscellaneous fexpr parser constructions for vecs and array kinds ([#6239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6239)); [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) opened a PR reverting a Flambda 2 array-element-kind meet bug fix that was masking a different root cause ([#6233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6233)).
- **Ongoing: SSA intermediate representation** — the large PR adding an SSA IR between Cmm and CFG ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) by [ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) received an update fixing an issue with dead trap-handler elimination, and remains in review.
- **Ongoing: layout polymorphism** — [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) continues work on merging uniform and mixed blocks ([#6224](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6224), draft) and layout polymorphic functions ([#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870)); [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) provided review on the latter.
- **Ongoing: ocamlfilt demangler** — [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) and [shym](https://github.com/shym) continued refining the pure-OCaml `ocamlfilt` demangler tool ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) with exception-safety improvements and design discussions.
- **5.4 merge work**: the upstream 5.4 merge branch saw substantial activity as [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) and [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) worked through the final merge conflict resolutions, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) reviewing. The branch was finally merged into main on June 12 ([#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325), [#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179)).
repositories.discussions_summary: New community issue proposing AVX-512 backend support; bug report for unboxed float corruption when a domain is resized.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) opened a detailed proposal for **AVX-512 support** in the amd64 SIMD backend ([#6257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6257)), covering EVEX encoding, ZMM registers, opmask (k0–k7) register class additions, and a phased landing plan. The proposal explicitly asks about design coordination before starting, and discusses Intel's AVX10 consolidation and its implications for width-parametric implementation.
- [Ty Overby](https://github.com/TyOverby) reported that **resizing a domain while an unboxed float is in a register clobbers the value** ([#6255](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6255)), with a minimal reproducer using the `parallel` library. The bug was observed in a scheduler's root domain but not on the main stack.
- [Jacob Van Buren](https://github.com/jvanburen) raised the question of whether the **K&R-style parameter lists** remaining in the OCaml runtime can simply be fixed ([#6217](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6217)). [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) confirmed the intent to eliminate them, noting most were addressed in a prior GCC 15 / Autoconf 2.7x migration.
- The ongoing **`ocamlfilt` demangler** PR ([#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) saw a detailed design discussion about handling redundant location chains in nested anonymous functions, missing functor application site information, and whether trailing `_code` suffixes and second stamps should be dropped from demangled output.
repositories.trends_summary: Post-5.4 merge consolidation drives activity, with parallel workstreams on unboxed types, mode improvements, runtime API, and name mangling tests.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge completion** marks a turning point: work is now shifting from merge logistics to post-merge cleanup, test promotion, and leveraging 5.4 features such as the compression module, updated stdlib, and runtime header hygiene changes still in open PRs ([#5533](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5533), [#6138](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6138)).
- **Unboxed types representation is moving toward stricter enforcement**: the emerging pattern of requiring explicit attributes like `[@all_void_constructor]` and restricting polymorphism over `any`-kinded constructors ([#6256](https://github.comml/oxcaml/issues/6256), [#6238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6238)) indicates preparatory work for changing the runtime representation of void-containing constructors.
- **Mode error messages are getting detailed source attribution**: multiple open PRs from [ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#6249](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6249), [#6205](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6205), [#6244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6244)) and from [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#6074](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6074)) show a concerted effort to make the mode/modality type error experience substantially better.
- **Name mangling test infrastructure is being consolidated**: the large [#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552) PR was split into four smaller targeted PRs this week, each adding test coverage for one aspect (zero-alloc, llvmize, upstream testsuite, mangling tests), enabling incremental merging.
- **Runtime API is expanding for external memory integration**: the draft `caml_add_extent` API ([#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)) and the `-alias` and `-nondep` compiler flag additions ([#6182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6182), [#6229](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6229)) suggest increasing investment in interoperability between OxCaml-managed and externally-managed memory and modules.
repositories.contributors_summary: 17 contributors were active across code, reviews, and discussions, with the 5.4 merge landing and post-merge work spanning nearly the whole team.
repositories.contributors:
- [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) drove the **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** to completion ([#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)), a multi-month effort finalising with a merge into main on June 12.
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) handled the final merge-upstream-5.4 branch integration ([#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179)), opened a revert PR for a Flambda 2 array-element-kind fix ([#6233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6233)), and continues the large DWARF inlined-parameter PR ([#5961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5961)).
- [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) fixed typing regressions introduced during 5.4 merge conflict resolution, including `@untagged` error messages and `solve_Pexp_field` unification ([#6247](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6247)), and provided review on layout polymorphic functions ([#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870)).

repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing expansion of the Dune 3.22.x package family into OxCaml, with attention to version constraint correctness for minus31 builds.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **Dune 3.22.x package family** is being progressively added to the OxCaml opam repository, with this week completing the remaining sub-packages following earlier additions; each package includes an `oxcaml-dune.patch` applied via the guard/patches-enabled pattern ([#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49))

- The shift by Dune from using the external `patch` binary to a **pure OCaml patch implementation** ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)) is surfacing downstream compatibility issues in OxCaml patch files, requiring upstream fixes in [ocaml/dune#15146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15146)

repositories.brief_summary: Socket creation now defaults to CLOEXEC; new PRs add RWF flags and vectored I/O bindings to ocaml-uring.
repositories.new_features_summary: Socket CLOEXEC default added; RWF per-operation flags and vectored fixed-buffer bindings proposed.
repositories.new_features:
- **Socket creation now defaults to `CLOEXEC`** in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151): the `socket` function's `?flags` optional argument was changed from `Sock_flags.empty` to `Sock_flags.cloexec`, preventing accidental fd leaks into child processes without requiring a separate `set_close_on_exec` call. The API change is backwards-compatible — callers who want no flags can pass `~flags:Sock_flags.empty` explicitly.
- **Per-operation RWF flags for read/write submissions** proposed in [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154): exposes the Linux `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (e.g. `RWF_DSYNC` to avoid a separate `fsync` after writes, and `RWF_NOCACHE` to skip polluting the buffer cache during large copies). These flags are available only on recent 6.x kernels and are intended for use in `eio_linux`.
- **Vectored read/write bindings for fixed buffers** proposed in [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155): corrects previously misnamed C stubs that were not actually performing vectored I/O, and adds proper `readv`/`writev` bindings for fixed-buffer operations.
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened for low-level I/O improvements; two older experimental PRs closed; bytes buffer migration ongoing.
repositories.activity:
- **New PR [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155)** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): adds correct vectored read/writev bindings for fixed buffers, fixing previously misnamed C stubs that were not performing vectored calls (172 additions, 20 deletions across 5 files).
- **New PR [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154)** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2` RWF flags at the uring layer, enabling callers like `eio_linux` to use `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_NOCACHE` to reduce unnecessary kernel operations.
- **Draft PR [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)** switching the buffer type from `bigarray`/`cstruct` to `bytes` is active with ongoing benchmarking: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) shared microbenchmark results showing no regression in filesystem I/O throughput, improved large-buffer allocation (avoids bigarray finalizer overhead), but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor needed to keep bytes immovable.
- **PR [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151)** merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): socket flags support landed with CLOEXEC as the new default, enabling [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843) to use uring socket creation directly.
- **PR [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)** (Linux errno module) closed without merging: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted the module has limited utility in Eio since `Unix_error` is always required, and suggested adding useful errnos upstream to OCaml instead. Discussion referenced [ocaml-multicore/eio#788](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/788) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851).
- **PR [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101)** (Cstruct-to-Bstruct experiment) closed by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) in favour of the more comprehensive bytes migration in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153).
repositories.discussions_summary: Benchmarking tradeoffs of bytes vs bigarray and errno module utility debated across PRs #152 and #153.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Bytes vs bigarray benchmarking** in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked whether the switch to `bytes` showed measurable improvement; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) shared results showing improved large-buffer allocation and I/O throughput when the caller already uses bytes (avoiding `Cstruct.to_bytes` conversion), but noted small buffers are worse due to the 2KB minimum allocation needed to prevent GC movement. The tradeoff suggests callers should coalesce small writes before submitting.
- **Errno module utility** in [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152): extensive discussion between [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on whether a Linux-specific errno module adds value given that `Unix_error` is always required for Eio's `Low_level` fallback paths. The conclusion was to close the PR and revisit only if specific errnos prove necessary, with a preference for adding useful ones upstream to OCaml rather than in ocaml-uring.
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to modernize ocaml-uring's API: safer defaults, bytes-backed buffers, and finer-grained Linux I/O control.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **API modernization toward safer defaults**: the CLOEXEC change in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) reflects a pattern of making secure behavior opt-out rather than opt-in, aligning with modern Linux socket best practices.
- **Migration away from Cstruct/bigarray toward OCaml `bytes`**: [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) and the closure of [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101) show a deliberate effort to reduce dependencies on C-backed memory types and improve interoperability with standard OCaml libraries that work with `bytes`.
- **Exposing more fine-grained Linux kernel features**: [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154) (RWF flags) and [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155) (vectored fixed-buffer I/O) continue the pattern of surfacing advanced `io_uring` and Linux kernel capabilities at the OCaml level for use by higher-level libraries like `eio_linux`.
repositories.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy drove all code changes this week; Thomas Leonard and Patrick Ferris contributed review and triage.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) merged [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) (socket CLOEXEC default), opened new PRs [#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154) (RWF flags) and [#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155) (vectored fixed-buffer bindings), continued work on draft [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) (bytes buffer migration) including sharing benchmark results, and participated in closing discussions for [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided review feedback on [#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152) (questioning the utility of the errno module and referencing [ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851) as an alternative), asked clarifying questions on [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) about benchmarking results, and discussed socket CLOEXEC default behavior in [#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151).
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) closed the long-standing experimental PR [#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101) (Cstruct-to-Bstruct) in favour of the newer bytes migration approach in [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153).

repositories.brief_summary: New incremental directory reading API and Path.open_subtree rename landed, with eio_linux scheduler simplification and ENOMEM resilience.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Path.with_dir_entries/read_dir_entries APIs for incremental directory reading; Path.open_subtree replaces open_dir.
repositories.new_features:
- **`Path.open_subtree`** and **`Path.with_subtree`** replace the old `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` functions with clearer names that communicate their confinement semantics — the new names make it explicit that these functions restrict access to a subtree, not merely open a directory ([#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))
- **`Path.with_dir_entries`** provides incremental directory entry reading, streaming `(File.Stat.kind * string)` pairs as a lazy `Seq.t` without loading all entries at once — this avoids sorting overhead and is significantly faster for large directories ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Path.read_dir_entries`** complements the above with a batch version that returns a sorted `(File.Stat.kind * string) list`, giving callers file type information alongside names without a separate `kind` call per entry ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Eio_linux.Low_level.read_some_dir`** exposes a new low-level function for reading batches of directory entries with their kind from the Linux backend, used by the incremental directory walker ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Eio_posix.Low_level.with_dir_entries`** provides the POSIX-backend implementation of incremental directory reading, with batched reads and proper worker-thread use for opening directories ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`eio_linux` now handles `ENOMEM` from `io_uring_queue_init`** by falling back to `eio_posix`, improving resilience on memory-constrained systems where the io_uring ring itself cannot be allocated ([#852](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/852))
- Updated **README.md** examples to use `open_subtree`/`with_subtree` in place of the old `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` names, keeping documentation consistent with the new API ([#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))
repositories.activity_summary: Three open PRs pending review cover ftruncate/fdatasync/uring networking ops, socket options, and chmod support.
repositories.activity:
- **`add ftruncate/fdatasync and use uring for fsync/shutdown/socket`** opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and awaiting review — adds `ftruncate`/`fdatasync` to the Linux backend and wires up newer io_uring operations for `fsync`, `shutdown`, and socket calls ([#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850))
- **`Eio_unix.Sockopt` module** for getting and setting socket options, originally opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) in 2023 and rebased this week — the PR adds a GADT-based interface to socket options and is seeking design feedback on the `SOCKET` module type integration ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575))
- **`chmod` support in `Eio_linux` and `Eio_posix`** from [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), rebased by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) this week with uring `nofollow` flag usage and `File.Unix_perm.t` type alignment — still open awaiting final review ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785))
- **`eio_linux` scheduler simplification** via moving read/write retries out of the scheduler into the calling fiber, removing the `Read`/`Write` job types, `file_offset` type, and `amount` type — merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849))
- **Removal of unused retry logic** in `eio_linux` fixed-buffer operations (EAGAIN/EINTR retries that can no longer trigger since switching to `DEFER_TASKRUN`) — merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851))
- Long-standing issue **"Occasional deadlocks with systhreads"** ([#700](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/700)) was closed this week
- Discussion on **issue #845** about disabling fixed buffers by default touched on a broader proposal to restrict the `eio_linux` uring backend to kernel 6.17+/7.x+, which would eliminate many op-probe checks for newer uring features ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845))
repositories.discussions_summary: Active debate on Path.open_subtree naming and whether the API should use types to distinguish confined vs. unconfined directory handles.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **`Path.open_subtree` rename** sparked substantive discussion in [#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853) about whether the new name adequately conveys confinement semantics; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) questioned if the confusion was fundamentally a documentation problem, while [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued `with_open_dir` sounded like a directory-listing API rather than a capability-confinement primitive — [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) noted that missing a call to `open_dir` in [mirage/ocaml-tar#177](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-tar/issues/177) was a concrete real-world example of the confusion
- Discussion raised the question of whether `Path.t` should use **distinct types** for confined vs. unconfined directory handles — [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued this is not practical because symlinks mean you cannot statically track whether traversal can escape, and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) proposed `Path.(/)` might be renamed to `Path.extend_unconfined` as an alternative signal at the use site ([#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))
- A discussion about **kernel version requirements for `eio_linux`** emerged in issue [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845), with [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) suggesting that restricting the uring backend to kernel 6.17+ or 7.x+ would eliminate many feature-probe calls needed for newer io_uring operations
- The **`with_dir_entries` implementation** discussion in [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821) included a deep dive into whether `caml_hash_variant` could be pre-computed at compile time — [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) tested in Compiler Explorer and found gcc/clang optimise it away, but noted this doesn't work in OCaml C stubs since `caml_hash_variant` is defined in a `.c` file rather than a header
repositories.trends_summary: Development is shifting toward richer filesystem APIs and clearer capability-confinement terminology while simplifying the Linux scheduler internals.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Filesystem API enrichment** is a clear theme, with `with_dir_entries`/`read_dir_entries` adding kind-aware directory reading and pending PRs adding `ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, `fsync`, `chmod`, and socket options — the API surface for filesystem and I/O operations is steadily expanding ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821), [#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850), [#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785))
- **Capability confinement terminology** is being tightened, with `open_subtree`/`with_subtree` replacing the less descriptive `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` — discussion suggests further renaming (`Path.(/)` → `Path.extend_unconfined`) may follow to make capability boundaries visible at use sites ([#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))
- **io_uring backend simplification** continues as the scheduler sheds special-cased logic (removing the Read/Write job types, retry loops, `file_offset` type), moving toward a leaner scheduler where fibers handle their own retry policy ([#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849), [#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851))
- A proposal to **require a minimum kernel version** for `eio_linux` (6.17+/7.x+) surfaced in discussion, which would enable removal of many op-probe workarounds and simplify the backend considerably ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845))
repositories.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove most merged work; Patrick Ferris and Anil Madhavapeddy contributed ongoing PRs, reviews, and discussion.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) merged four PRs this week: the `Path.open_subtree` rename ([#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)), incremental directory reading ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) after rebasing and significantly extending [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s original work, ENOMEM handling for io_uring ([#852](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/852)), and removal of unused fixed-buffer retry code ([#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851)); he also merged the scheduler simplification ([#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)) and participated in detailed design discussions on confinement naming and C hash evaluation
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) originally authored the incremental directory reading PR ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) including benchmarks showing 10–13× speedups on Linux for recursive directory walks, and has the `chmod` support PR ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)) under active review; he also contributed to the naming discussion around `open_subtree` with a real-world security example from the `ocaml-tar` project
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened the `ftruncate`/`fdatasync`/uring networking PR ([#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)), rebased the long-standing `Sockopt` PR ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575)) and `chmod` PR ([#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)), reviewed and approved merges, and engaged in discussions on both the `open_subtree` naming debate and the proposal to raise the minimum kernel version requirement for `eio_linux` ([#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845))

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 landed in opam-repository with 25+ new packages; ocaml.org added AI dev docs and a wasm_of_ocaml WASI backstage post.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: opam-repository added OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 and 25+ packages; ocaml.org fixed Workshop UI contrast and published a wasm_of_ocaml WASI article.
group_summaries.new_features:
**ocaml/opam-repository**
- [OCaml 5.5.0~rc1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30023) packaged `ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, and `ocaml-variants...+options` with runtime fixes and a Windows filename quoting security fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#30023](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30023))
- **awskit** (10 packages, 0.1.0) — AWS infrastructure library with signing, credentials, S3 client, and Eio/Lwt adapters ([ocaml/opam-repository#29980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29980))
- **migra** (1.0.0, 1.0.1, 2.0.0) — database migration tool for PostgreSQL, MariaDB/MySQL, and SQLite with checksum validation ([ocaml/opam-repository#30002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30002), [#30012](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30012), [#30033](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30033))
- **mnet** (0.0.3, 6 packages) — TCP implementation for Miou and Solo5 unikernels, now including `mnet-ssh` for SSH in unikernels ([ocaml/opam-repository#30011](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30011))
- **cabal** (0.1.0) — backend-agnostic agent execution primitives for OCaml applications driving agentic CLI backends ([ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008))
- **ocaml-ai-sdk** (0.4, 2 packages) — provider abstraction for AI models with Anthropic and OpenRouter prompt caching support ([ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005))
- **transom** (0.2.0) — OCaml backend for desktop WebView apps with typed IPC and generated glue code ([ocaml/opam-repository#30037](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30037))
- **contract** (0.2.0) — typed HTTP API contract library with OpenAPI 3.0.3 output ([ocaml/opam-repository#30014](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30014))
- **doctor** (0.4.0) — diagnostic CLI checking opam, dune, LSP, and editor integration for OCaml development environments ([ocaml/opam-repository#30015](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30015))
- **liquid-ml** (0.1.4, 5 packages) — Shopify Liquid templating language implementation in OCaml ([ocaml/opam-repository#30004](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30004))
- **melange-edn** (0.1.0 and 0.3.0) — EDN reader and writer for Melange targeting JavaScript ([ocaml/opam-repository#29999](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29999), [#30028](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30028))
- **kind2** (3.0.0) — SMT-based automatic model checker for Lustre programs ([ocaml/opam-repository#30019](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30019))
- **notty-miou** (0.0.2, 4 packages) — terminal library using Miou with new mkernel/mnet support ([ocaml/opam-repository#30027](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30027))
- **miou** (0.7.0) — composable concurrency primitives adding `Miou.get` and `Miou.Promise.state` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009))
- **caqti** and **caqti-lwt** (2.3.2) — database access library fixing timed cleanup of idle connection pool entries ([ocaml/opam-repository#30026](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30026))
- **Frama-C** (32.1-Germanium) — static analysis platform for C ([ocaml/opam-repository#30016](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30016))
- **goblint-cil** (2.1.0) — C frontend for program analysis with cast kinds, `_Static_assert` checking, and attribute handling improvements ([ocaml/opam-repository#30022](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30022))
- **hxd** (0.5.0) — hexdump library with new introspection API for `cfg` values ([ocaml/opam-repository#30010](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30010))
- **ca-certs-nss** (3.125) — X.509 trust anchors updated to NSS 3.125 (2026-06-11) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30038](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30038))
- **checked_oint** (2.0.1) — library for checked integer arithmetic ([ocaml/opam-repository#30031](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30031))
- **timedesc** and related (3.1.2, 6 packages) — OCaml datetime handling library suite ([ocaml/opam-repository#29995](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29995))
- **mcrunch** (0.0.3) — file crunching tool now handling empty files ([ocaml/opam-repository#30025](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30025))
- **dockerfile** (8.4.1, 3 packages) — Dockerfile eDSL with a libzstd packaging fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#30018](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30018))
- **mingw-w64-shims** (1.0.1) — Windows shims package with clarified `conf-` usage ([ocaml/opam-repository#30040](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30040))
- **embedded_ocaml_templates** (0.8.1) — template library making `ppx_inline_test` a test-only dependency ([ocaml/opam-repository#29929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29929))

**ocaml/ocaml.org**
- Fixed **contrast on OCaml Workshop 2026 date pills** — switched from `text-title` to `text-white` on orange background badges across both `/ocaml-workshop-2026` and `/ocaml-workshop-2026/cfp` pages ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3673](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3673))
- Added **`CLAUDE.md` developer guide** with AI-assisted development context covering build commands, architecture, EML templating, data flow, dependency pinning, and CI/deployment ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))
- Expanded **`HACKING.md`** with a "Before Submitting a PR" section, updated `tool/` directory tree, and corrected dependency pinning file list ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))
- Published **Backstage article on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI prototype** covering WASI preview1 backend, CPS effects compilation, and a call for production users or sponsors ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))
- Added seven new **OCaml Planet blog posts** via automated scraping across three merged PRs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3674](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3674), [#3676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3676), [#3678](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3678))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) had its highest-volume week in months: OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 landed via [ocaml/opam-repository#30023](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30023), accompanied by 25+ new or updated packages spanning AWS SDKs, database tooling, unikernel networking, AI/agent libraries, static analysis, and developer tooling
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) saw lighter activity focused on content and developer documentation: UI contrast fixes, a new `CLAUDE.md` guide, a backstage post on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI, and seven new Planet blog posts
- Infrastructure work in opam-repository hardened Windows CI ([ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998)), updated `conf-*` packages for 0install solver compatibility on Windows ([ocaml/opam-repository#30029](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30029)), and addressed dune 3.24 install layout changes affecting multiple packages
- The [Miou/robur-coop](https://github.com/mirage) unikernel ecosystem had a coordinated release week: `miou`, `mnet`, `mcrunch`, and `notty-miou` all landed together
- Four ocaml.org PRs remain open pending review: platform newsletter, `llms.txt` endpoint, GC tutorial clarification, and AWS industrial users addition
group_summaries.activity_summary: Windows CI hardened, dune 3.24 compatibility fixes applied across three packages, and four ocaml.org content PRs await merge.
group_summaries.activity:
**ocaml/opam-repository infrastructure and compatibility**
- **Windows CI hardened**: [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998) updated CI to install edited packages by upgrading from the master version first, catching state regressions and lockfile issues; includes a lint error 46 suppression for `conf`-flagged packages with install sections
- **`conf-*` packages updated** for 0install solver compatibility on Windows, tweaking many `conf-*` packages and adding `conf-pkg-config.5` via [ocaml/opam-repository#30029](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30029)
- **dune 3.24 compatibility**: tests for `crunch.4.0.0` [ocaml/opam-repository#30035](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30035), `lintcstubs.0.4.7` [ocaml/opam-repository#30032](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30032), and `merlin` [ocaml/opam-repository#30044](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30044) conditionally gated on `dune < 3.24` due to install layout changes
- **OxCaml compatibility**: [ocaml/opam-repository#30041](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30041) adds missing constraints to `async_websocket.v0.13.0` to prevent selection in OxCaml switches
- **Dependency fixes**: `mazeppa.0.7.2`'s `checked_oint` dependency corrected via [ocaml/opam-repository#30034](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30034); `diffast-core` 0.2/0.4 incompatibility patched via [ocaml/opam-repository#30039](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30039)
- **Pending submissions**: `syguslib-utils.0.3.0` (SyGuS Language parser) [ocaml/opam-repository#30043](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30043); `store.0.2`, `store-dict.0.1`, `store-pushqueue.0.1` [ocaml/opam-repository#30024](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30024); `diffast.0.4` stalled on Cygwin linker errors and a RISC-V segfault [ocaml/opam-repository#30013](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30013); `ocaml-r.0.7.0` pending macOS failure resolution [ocaml/opam-repository#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30007); `lstar-rocq.1.0` (formally-verified L* in Rocq) [ocaml/opam-repository#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29883)

**ocaml/ocaml.org content pipeline**
- **Platform newsletter (Feb-May 2026)** opened by [@sabine](https://github.com/sabine) covering OCaml Platform updates ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3680](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3680))
- **`llms.txt` endpoint** (open since March) proposed by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) adding a ~90-link markdown index served at `/llms.txt` for LLM-friendly site discovery ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3579))
- **Garbage collector tutorial clarification** by [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) noting that `Gc.tune` examples rely on Jane Street's Core library, pointing to `Gc.get`/`Gc.set` as the standard-library alternative ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3682](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3682))
- **AWS added to industrial users page** by [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) documenting AWS's use of HOL Light for formal verification of s2n-bignum in AWS-LC ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3681](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3681))
- Two automated **platform release scrape PRs** remain open pending merge ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3677](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3677), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3679](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3679))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **AI and agent tooling across both repos**: opam-repository added `ocaml-ai-sdk.0.4` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005)) and `cabal.0.1.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008)) targeting AI/LLM use cases, while ocaml.org added a `CLAUDE.md` guide ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585)) formalising AI-assisted development; several commits in both repos are co-authored with Claude models
- **WebAssembly ecosystem coverage**: the ocaml.org backstage post on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667)) coincides with continued MirageOS/unikernel package activity in opam-repository (`mnet` [ocaml/opam-repository#30011](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30011), `miou` [ocaml/opam-repository#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009)), reflecting parallel progress on OCaml's systems/embedded stack
- **dune 3.24 install layout changes** surfaced in opam-repository this week ([ocaml/opam-repository#30035](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30035), [#30032](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30032), [#30044](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30044)) and will likely require follow-up documentation or guidance on ocaml.org as more packages are affected
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/opam-repository#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30007) (`ocaml-r.0.7.0`): [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) flagged macOS and older-distribution failures due to `conf-r-mathlib` being too old, suggesting merging anyway and updating the conf package separately
- [ocaml/opam-repository#30013](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30013) (`diffast.0.4`): [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) and the author discussed Cygwin linking errors and a RISC-V-specific segfault with OCaml 4.14; recommendation is to add an `available` constraint requiring OCaml 5+ on RISC-V
- [ocaml/opam-repository#30041](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30041): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened a PR to prevent OxCaml switches from selecting `async_websocket.v0.13.0`, noting it might also be reasonable to mark it `available: false`
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noted that Claude helped write the PowerShell CI scripts, with a wry observation about the experience
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3683](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3683): [Yuta Tokoi](https://github.com/yutatokoi) opened an issue reporting an apparent syntax error in "A Tour of OCaml" (a `#` accidentally prepended to an example) and resolved it the same day upon realising the error was in their own input
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Miou/robur-coop unikernel ecosystem** had a coordinated release week: `miou.0.7.0` [ocaml/opam-repository#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009), `mnet.0.0.3` [ocaml/opam-repository#30011](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30011), `mcrunch.0.0.3` [ocaml/opam-repository#30025](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30025), and `notty-miou.0.0.2` [ocaml/opam-repository#30027](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30027) all landed together, reflecting active development of OCaml unikernel networking infrastructure
- **AI/agent tooling** growing across the ecosystem: `ocaml-ai-sdk.0.4` [ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005), `cabal.0.1.0` [ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008), and `transom.0.2.0` [ocaml/opam-repository#30037](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30037) all target AI-adjacent or agentic use cases; `CLAUDE.md` on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585)) reflects AI-assisted development becoming a first-class workflow
- **dune 3.24 install layout changes** are creating recurring compatibility work: `crunch` [ocaml/opam-repository#30035](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30035), `lintcstubs` [ocaml/opam-repository#30032](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30032), and `merlin` [ocaml/opam-repository#30044](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30044) all required conditional test gating this week
- **OxCaml compatibility** is becoming a recurring concern: packages are being patched with `available` constraints to prevent selection in OxCaml switches, as seen in [ocaml/opam-repository#30041](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30041)
- **WebAssembly coverage** on ocaml.org is expanding: the backstage post on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI prototype ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667)) follows earlier wasm_of_ocaml coverage and tracks the maturing Wasm 3.0 standardisation (GC, tail calls, exception handling)

group_summaries.brief_summary: js_of_ocaml lands WASI runtime and 13+ bug fixes; dune advances RPC architecture; odoc adds OxCaml modalities; merlin prepares for OCaml 5.5.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: WASI 0.1 in wasm_of_ocaml, OxCaml modalities in odoc, Configurator pkg-config variable queries in dune, and let-punning locate fix in merlin.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **WASI 0.1 runtime** added to `wasm_of_ocaml` via `--enable wasi` flag ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon); supports Wizard engine and wasmtime, including WASI for separate compilation and exception handling
- **Documentation migrated** in js_of_ocaml from wiki format to odoc + wodoc ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)) by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat); unified under `dune build @doc`, published to gh-pages
- **OxCaml modalities** now rendered across all odoc output backends (HTML, LaTeX, man pages, markdown), resolving [ocaml/odoc#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416) via [ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- **`zero_alloc` arity annotation** omitted when it matches function arity, reducing visual noise in generated docs ([ocaml/odoc#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- **Configurator `query_variable`** adds `Pkg_config.query_variable` to query arbitrary pkg-config variables per package ([ocaml/dune#15078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15078)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), closing [ocaml/dune#3332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3332)
- **Memo API expansion**: new `Node.Packed` existential type ([ocaml/dune#15141](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15141)), `Spec` and `Table.spec` ([ocaml/dune#15143](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15143)), `node.mli` interface ([ocaml/dune#15124](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15124)), and `Deps.For_debugging.to_dyn` ([ocaml/dune#15102](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15102)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Memo parallel combinators**: `all_concurrently_unit`, `parallel_iter_set`, and `Map.parallel_iter` added ([ocaml/dune#15149](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15149)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`Fiber.Pool.with_`** helper for pool lifecycle management around RPC sessions ([ocaml/dune#15207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15207)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **`env_vars` alias** for environment variable stanzas ([ocaml/dune#15187](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15187)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **Let-punning locate fix** in merlin: `locate` on punned bindings like `let x in` or `let+ x in` now jumps to the source binding correctly, resolving [ocaml/merlin#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796) and [ocaml/merlin#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1579) via [ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)
- **js_of_ocaml runtime correctness fixes** (13 merged): `Ephemeron.blit_key` data corruption ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2264](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2264)), `Int64.shift_right` sign extension ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2278](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2278)), BLOCK32 unsigned shift ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2275](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2275)), `Sys.rename`-to-self deletion ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2268](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2268)), `Open_append` seek and write-access bugs ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2265](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2265), [ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2267](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2267)), `Printf` `#` flag on zero ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2266](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2266)), Str SIMPLEOPT infinite loop ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2277)), big-endian double array unmarshalling ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2276](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2276)), `flush` on closed channel ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2269](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2269)), `caml_dynlink_open_lib` mode argument ([ocaml/js_of_ocaml#2294](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2294))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune): Heavy RPC architecture refactoring and Memo API expansion dominated; new Configurator `query_variable` API merged ([ocaml/dune#15078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15078)), plus package lock directory fixes and a wave of documentation improvements across dozens of PRs
