- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam): Multiple PRs advanced (`.install` file extensions ([ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)), API dead-code cleanup ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)), `OpamTar` module ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945))); one CI fix merged ([ocaml/opam#6961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6961))
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): Let-punning locate fix merged ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)); draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade PR opened ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)); dune 3.24 path compatibility fix merged ([ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078))
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp): Hover-on-warnings PR ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606)) under review; structural search integration proposal ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) generated maintainer discussion
- [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn): No activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: dune RPC/Memo refactoring, opam .install improvements and API cleanup, merlin OCaml 5.5 prep, and js_of_ocaml systematic runtime fixes drove the week.
group_summaries.activity:
- **dune RPC server refactoring** ([ocaml/dune#15211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15211), [ocaml/dune#15202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15202), [ocaml/dune#15209](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15209), [ocaml/dune#15208](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15208)): session object replaced with a record, `Rpc.Global` indirection removed, server creation moved to socket creation time, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **dune Build_loop API redesign** ([ocaml/dune#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191)): `submit_rpc_request`, `cancel_rpc_requests_by_session`, and `sticky_goal` parameter introduced to enable independent completion of concurrent RPC builds, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **dune Memo internal refactoring**: renamed `Cell` to `Node`, `Make_parallel_map` to `Map`, moved invalidation into its own module ([ocaml/dune#15135](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15135), [ocaml/dune#15139](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15139), [ocaml/dune#15140](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15140)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **dune package lock directory fix**: nil arguments with always-false filters (previously left as `(when false "")`) are now dropped at assembly time ([ocaml/dune#15128](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15128)) by [Chizy](https://github.com/chizy7)
- **dune pkgconf `--personality` version guard**: now only passed when pkgconf ≥ 1.7.0, fixing breakage on RHEL 8 ([ocaml/dune#15142](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15142)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **dune `dune init` module name validation** added early validation for project module names ([ocaml/dune#15014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15014)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **dune documentation**: clarified `dune exec` argument separators ([ocaml/dune#15131](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15131)), `@doc` output ([ocaml/dune#15129](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15129)), executable linking modes ([ocaml/dune#15184](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15184)), build profile semantics ([ocaml/dune#15051](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15051)), and alias-only rules ([ocaml/dune#15069](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15069)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- **opam `init --bare` performance fix** ([ocaml/opam#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957)): prevents unnecessary compiler selection when no compiler is needed, fixing [ocaml/opam#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815), by [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz)
- **opam `OpamFilename` prefix bug fix** ([ocaml/opam#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6953)): `starts_with` and `dir_starts_with` no longer treat `foo/bar` as a prefix of `foo/bar-baz`, fixing [ocaml/opam#6948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6948), by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)
- **opam CI test reproducibility fix** ([ocaml/opam#6961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6961)): repository HTTP reftest no longer embeds timestamps in `opam admin index` output, eliminating Windows CI minute-boundary failures, by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- **opam API dead-code removal** ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)): 366 unused exported values, fields, and constructors removed by [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio); a handful restored after CI identified usage in `opam-rt`, `opam-publish`, and `opam-bundle`
- **opam `OpamTar` module** ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)): new module using the `tar` OCaml library instead of the system `tar` tool for archive creation, marked ready-to-merge, by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- **merlin draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade** ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)): 462-file draft PR opened by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) to keep Merlin in sync with upstream compiler
- **merlin dune 3.24 path compatibility** ([ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078)): no-dune test switched to `which ocamlmerlin` to avoid relying on dune internal install paths; identified via reverse-dependency tests in [ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996), by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **js_of_ocaml Wasm runtime cleanup** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262), draft): uses `$wasi` preprocessor variables, removes Binaryen tuples and pop instructions, adds missing function names for `wax` tool, by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **js_of_ocaml newly opened runtime fix PRs** from systematic review [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270): exact float formatting ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280)), float array hashing ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2283](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2283)), filesystem fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2286](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2286)), Unix fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2287](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2287)), Domain.spawn exception handling ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2291](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2291)), Graphics arc/plot fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2296](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2296)), and others, all by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
- **odoc CI lower-bound fix** for ocamlfind (`>= 1.9.8` in `with-test`) resolved RISC-V CI build failures ([ocaml/odoc#1446](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1446)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- **odoc `odoc-bench.opam` removal** ([ocaml/odoc#1447](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1447)): benchmarking setup removed as it had not been running and pulled unnecessary dependencies, by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune 3.24 layout changes affect merlin**: the upcoming dune [ocaml/dune#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432) install layout change broke merlin's no-dune test; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) diagnosed and fixed it in [ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078), identified via opam reverse-dependency tests in [ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996)
- **opam `.install` prefix sections blocked by opam review, awaited by dune**: [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)'s [ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) (adding `prefix`/`prefixexec` sections) is queued behind [ocaml/opam#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956); Ward explicitly asked about release timeline for dune to begin depending on the feature
- **js_of_ocaml docs migration uses odoc**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261) adopted odoc + wodoc for documentation, following odoc's own development; the CI lower-bound fix in [ocaml/odoc#1446](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1446) (bumping ocamlfind to `>= 1.9.8`) was relevant to RISC-V test environments shared across OCaml tooling projects
- **OCaml 5.5 readiness across merlin and js_of_ocaml**: [ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077) (462-file draft upgrade) and js_of_ocaml's release tracking [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241) both target the imminent OCaml 5.5.0 release, with coordinated timing
- **Ulysse active in both merlin and ocaml-lsp**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed [ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066) and [ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078) while also reviewing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606), providing cross-cutting context on compiler internals for both projects
- **David Allsopp active in both dune and opam**: fixed pkgconf `--personality` in dune ([ocaml/dune#15142](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15142)) and opened a `opam lint` warning-demotion request in opam ([ocaml/opam#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964)), motivated by [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998)
- **Rudi Grinberg active in both dune and ocaml-lsp**: dominated dune development this week while also weighing in on [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) regarding the appropriate architecture for structural search integration
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **js_of_ocaml systematic runtime review** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed comprehensive reviews of all `runtime/js/*.js` and `runtime/wasm/*.wat` files using differential testing (BigInt oracles with 200k/20k cases, RFC test vectors for MD5/BLAKE2, 300-case zstd differential, fuzzed mlBytes/UTF-8 codec tests), cataloguing 30+ bugs across correctness, crashes, and JS/Wasm parity
- **ocamlgrep structural search proposal** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating typed-AST structural search into ocaml-lsp; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) argued editor plugins are a better fit than the LSP server; work supervised by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) at LexiFi
- **opam solver downgrade behavior** ([ocaml/opam discussion #6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966)): [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) questioned why `opam upgrade` proposed downgrading `ansifmt` from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 to upgrade `re` to 1.14.0, raising questions about whether the default solver handles semantic versioning expectations appropriately
- **dune absolute path audit** ([ocaml/dune#12230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12230)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened sub-issues tracking commands that crash or misbehave on absolute paths (`dune utop`, `dune ocaml top`, `dune show targets`, `dune fmt` over RPC), each with a companion test PR
- **dune documentation gap collection** ([ocaml/dune#15153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15153)): a user visiting Inria collected feedback on documentation shortcomings; many of the linked issues were closed this week as documentation PRs landed
- **merlin locate on functor types** ([ocaml/merlin#1610](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1610)): [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted new location information in the typedtree for longidents may enable improvement, though the path is unclear
- **odoc polymorphic variant inherit doc-comments** ([ocaml/odoc#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846)): [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) traced the issue to the OCaml compiler's typed tree not carrying the attribute for `Rinherit`; upstream compiler work in [ocaml/ocaml#1903](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/1903) is a prerequisite
- **js_of_ocaml compiler output soundness issues** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2285](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2285)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) identified unsound optimisations in `js_simpl.ml` and `js_traverse.ml` — if-fusion collapsing effectful expressions and constant-if folding dropping `var` declarations, causing `ReferenceError` in strict/ESM contexts
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **RPC architecture hardening in dune**: the `Build_loop` API redesign ([ocaml/dune#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191)) separating RPC-triggered from file-watch-triggered builds and the `Fiber.Pool.with_` helper ([ocaml/dune#15207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15207)) indicate sustained investment in robust concurrent watch-mode handling
- **Memo surface expansion for tooling**: the week's Memo feature PRs (`Node.Packed` ([ocaml/dune#15141](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15141)), `Spec` ([ocaml/dune#15143](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15143)), `Deps.For_debugging` ([ocaml/dune#15102](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15102)), parallel combinators ([ocaml/dune#15149](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15149))) suggest preparation for richer external tooling or introspection use cases
- **Systematic AI-assisted correctness reviews** in js_of_ocaml: both [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270) use a review-plus-differential-testing methodology (BigInt oracles, RFC vectors, fuzz runs), driving clusters of 15+ PRs rather than individual one-off fixes
- **JS/Wasm runtime convergence** in js_of_ocaml: many bug fixes explicitly cross-reference divergences between `runtime/js/*.js` and `runtime/wasm/*.wat`, with the Wasm cleanup PR [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262) aiming to enable the same systematic review methodology for Wasm files
- **OxCaml tooling support expanding**: odoc modalities rendering ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and the ongoing `include functor` support ([ocaml/odoc#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419)) tracked under [ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) show steady progress in documenting OxCaml-specific constructs
- **Proactive upstream compatibility**: merlin's OCaml 5.5 upgrade ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)) and dune path fix ([ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078)) were driven by upstream changes before any breakage reached users, coordinated with js_of_ocaml's 5.5 release tracking ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241))
- **Dependency reduction as a CI health strategy**: `ppx_expect` removed from odoc tests ([ocaml/odoc#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)), `odoc-bench.opam` dropped ([ocaml/odoc#1447](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1447)), and opam's API dead-code removal ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)) all reduce the footprint for faster, more reliable CI
- **Editor tooling reaching deeper into compiler internals**: both ocaml-lsp hover-on-warnings ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1606)) and structural search ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) involve surfacing typed compiler artifacts (warning metadata, typed ASTs) in the editor, reflecting a broader push to expose more of the OCaml compiler's analysis in IDE tooling
- **opam `.install` file format receiving coordinated attention**: [ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) (new sections), [ocaml/opam#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956) (exhaustive tests), and [ocaml/opam#6958](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6958)/[ocaml/opam#6959](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6959) (error message improvements) form a cluster of coordinated improvements, with dune as a downstream consumer awaiting the new sections

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 5.4 upstream merge landed in oxcaml/oxcaml; Dune 3.22.2+ox packages added to oxcaml/opam-repository.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.4 merge lands; 16 Dune 3.22.2+ox packages added; runtime fixes for lazy atomicity and OCAMLRUNPARAM.
group_summaries.new_features:
- The **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) landed on June 12, bringing in the full 5.4 branch including stdlib updates, runtime changes, and toolchain improvements, accompanied by a magic number bump.
- **Correct handling of concurrent lazy header marking** in the major GC, backported from [ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856): fixes potential SEGVs or heap corruption when two domains race on a lazy block ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6228)).
- **Graceful degradation for `OCAMLRUNPARAM=d=` values** exceeding valid range, avoiding a fatal error in the runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6215](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6215)).
- **Post-5.4-merge typing fixes** by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin): corrected `@untagged` error messages on externals with `int8`/`int16`, updated format strings in `typetexp.ml`, and unified duplicate `solve_Pexp_field`/`type_label_projection` functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6247](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6247)).
- **CFG merge-blocks register equality strengthened**: structural equality now used instead of physical equality in the merge-blocks pass ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6223](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6223)).
- **Merlin updated to 5.2.0minus-40** with a merlin-specific diff added to `out_type.ml` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6211](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6211), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6216](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6216), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6225](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6225)).
- **Dune 3.22.2+ox package family** (16 sub-packages including `chrome-trace`, `dune-build-info`, `dune-rpc`, `stdune`, `xdg`, etc.) added to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) with `oxcaml-dune.patch` compatibility ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49)).
- **Version constraint fixes** in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) for `stdune`, `extlib`, `eio_linux`, `cmarkit`, `omd`, `yojson`, and `cmdliner` to correctly scope `5.2.0minus31` vs `5.2.0minus31+` builds ([oxcaml/opam-repository#52](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/52)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)** had the highest activity: the OCaml 5.4 upstream merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) completed on June 12 after months of work, triggering 40+ new PRs for post-merge cleanup, unboxed types, mode error improvements, runtime API, and name mangling test infrastructure.
- **[oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)** completed the Dune 3.22.2+ox package additions ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49)) and merged accumulated constraint fixes ([oxcaml/opam-repository#52](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/52)), while [oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) remains open pending an upstream Dune fix.
- **[oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)** saw a large draft PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) for merging minus-37 into the 5.4 branch closed without merging due to conflicts across 896 files.
- **[oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml)** and **[janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository)** had no recorded activity this week.
- 17+ contributors were active across the group, concentrated in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Post-5.4 merge wave opens 40+ PRs across types, runtime, name mangling, and Merlin sync; opam packages and constraint fixes merged.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Mode error reporting improvements** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6249](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6249), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6227](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6227)) from [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) add annotation-hint source locations and rework subsumption error printing; a fix to `Const.comonadic_to_monadic_min` causing incorrect staticity errors in loops also landed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6244)).
- **Unboxed types representation work** from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): requiring `[@all_void_constructor]` on void-argument constructors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256)), fixing inline record representation for `any` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6240)), and restricting variant manipulation to types with determined layouts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6238)).
- **Runtime API addition**: [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a draft introducing `caml_add_extent` for handing external memory regions to the GC ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)), a ZMM28 save/restore fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6252](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6252)), and a PR to retire runtime 4 across 2,000+ files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218)).
- **Compiler flag additions**: [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) opened draft PRs for `-nondep <module>` to erase modules from `.cmi` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6229](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6229)) and `-Ix` for dynamic staticity enforcement ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6226](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6226)).
- **Name mangling test infrastructure**: [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) split [oxcaml/oxcaml#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552) into four targeted PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6234](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6234), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6235), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6236](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6236), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6237)); [@shym](https://github.com/shym) added structured mangling improvements for nested anonymous functions and modules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6242)).
- **Merlin sync in oxcaml/oxcaml**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a CI enforcement script ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)), ikinds default fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6241](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6241)), and 5.4 Merlin upgrade PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)).
- **Stdlib and typing**: [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) opened PRs changing stdlib parameterised types to accept `any` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6253](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6253)), fixing a `transl_idx` crash with `any` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6246](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6246)), and allowing non-`value` optional arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6219](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6219)).
- **Fexpr and Flambda 2**: [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) added method call support ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6222](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6222)) and fexpr parser fixes for vecs and array kinds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6239](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6239)); [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) reverted a Flambda 2 array-element-kind meet bug fix masking a different root cause ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6233](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6233)).
- **SSA IR** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) received an update fixing dead trap-handler elimination and remains under review.
- **Layout polymorphism**: [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) continues work on merging uniform and mixed blocks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6224](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6224)) and layout polymorphic functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870)).
- **Effects and modular explicits**: [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) opened PRs forcing effect pattern-matching to legacy mode ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6230](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6230)) and importing modular explicits from upstream ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6243](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6243)).
- **`ocamlfilt` demangler** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)): [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) and [@shym](https://github.com/shym) continued exception-safety improvements and design discussions on nested anonymous function handling.
- **oxcaml/merlin**: Large draft merge PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) for minus-37 into the 5.4 branch was closed on June 9 after encountering conflicts across 896 files (298k additions, 51k deletions).
- **oxcaml/opam-repository**: Dune 3.22.2+ox packages merged ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49)); open PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51)) for `ignore-opam.patch` line number fix awaits resolution of [ocaml/dune#15146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15146).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin synchronisation across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a CI enforcement script (`assert-in-sync.sh`) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) to keep Merlin in sync with frontend changes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)), while [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) separately closed a large draft merge PR in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) that was too conflicted to land; the 5.4 Merlin upgrade PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) coordinates this effort.
- **Dune compatibility chain from [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)**: Dune's shift to a pure OCaml patch implementation ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)) broke OxCaml patch files; [oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) awaits upstream resolution via [ocaml/dune#15146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15146), with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) bridging both discussions.
- **[David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) active across two repositories**: drives constraint and layout fixes in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) ([oxcaml/opam-repository#52](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/52)) while also working on the OCaml 5.4 Compression module in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5533](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5533)).
- **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) drives opam packaging work**: the landed 5.4 merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) and associated Merlin upgrade will likely require further [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) package additions for the new `5.4.0+ox1` compiler variant referenced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) opened a detailed proposal for **AVX-512 support** in the amd64 SIMD backend ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6257)), covering EVEX encoding, ZMM register classes, opmask (k0–k7) additions, and a phased landing plan, explicitly requesting design coordination before implementation.
- [Ty Overby](https://github.com/TyOverby) reported that **resizing a domain while an unboxed float is in a register clobbers the value** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6255](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6255)), with a minimal reproducer using the `parallel` library.
- [Jacob Van Buren](https://github.com/jvanburen) raised whether **K&R-style parameter lists** in the OCaml runtime can be removed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6217](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6217)); [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) confirmed intent to eliminate them, with most already addressed in a prior GCC 15 / Autoconf 2.7x migration.
- The **`ocamlfilt` demangler** PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5100)) saw detailed discussion on handling redundant location chains in nested anonymous functions, missing functor application site information, and whether trailing `_code` suffixes and second stamps should be dropped.
- In [oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) debated **tactical patching vs. upstream fix** for `ignore-opam.patch` line drift, with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) providing a `dune-project` workaround for the `oxcaml-dune`/`dune` solver conflict.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Post-5.4 merge consolidation**: the completion of [oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325) marks a shift from merge logistics to post-merge cleanup, test promotion, and leveraging 5.4 features such as the Compression module ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5533](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5533)) and updated runtime header hygiene ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6138](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6138)).
- **Unboxed types moving toward stricter enforcement**: the pattern of requiring `[@all_void_constructor]` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256)) and restricting polymorphism over `any`-kinded constructors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6238)) indicates preparatory work for changing the runtime representation of void-containing constructors.
- **Mode error messages gaining detailed source attribution**: multiple open PRs from [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6249](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6249), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6205](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6205), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6244)) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6074](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6074)) show a concerted effort to improve mode/modality type error diagnostics.
- **Name mangling test infrastructure consolidation**: the large [oxcaml/oxcaml#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552) was split into four smaller PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6234](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6234), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6235), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6236](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6236), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6237)) to enable incremental merging.
- **Runtime API expanding for external memory integration**: the draft `caml_add_extent` API ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)) and the `-alias`/`-nondep` compiler flags ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6182), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6229](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6229)) indicate increasing investment in OxCaml/external memory interoperability.
- **Dune 3.22.x package family expansion in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)**: the completion of the 16-package Dune 3.22.2+ox family ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49)) and ongoing constraint precision fixes ([oxcaml/opam-repository#52](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/52)) reflect a maturing package ecosystem for OxCaml.
- **Merlin synchronisation becoming a formal CI requirement**: the `assert-in-sync.sh` script ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)) signals a move from ad-hoc to automated enforcement of frontend/Merlin consistency, motivated by the complexity of the 5.4 merge.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Two CVE security fixes landed in ocaml/ocaml alongside a GC mark_header regression fix and Flambda miscompilation correction.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: CVE fixes for Windows filename quoting and intern.c, runtime_events infinite loop prevention, and Flambda stub inlining fix.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Fixed shell injection vulnerability in `Filename.quote_command` on Windows (CVE-2026-41083, OSEC-2026-05): filenames with special characters like `&` in redirection arguments were not properly quoted, potentially allowing unintended command execution. [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) implemented the fix, reviewed by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14853](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14853))
- Fixed inverted check in `mark_header` for lazy tags — a regression from [ocaml/ocaml#14816](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14816) that could cause corruption and segfaults in multi-domain programs using laziness, verified by [Sadiq Jaffer](https://github.com/sadiqj) and [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) ([ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856)) by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- Fixed infinite loop in `runtime_events.c`'s `write_to_ring` when an event exceeds the configured ring buffer size; the runtime now emits a fatal error suggesting the minimum required `e=` value (Michael Bacarella, review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [ocaml/ocaml#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- Fixed infinite recursive inlining of stub functions with Flambda, a miscompilation discovered by Nathanaëlle Courant and Vincent Laviron, fixed by Pierre Chambart ([ocaml/ocaml#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828), [ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839))
- Fixed modular explicit type expansion to correctly update expansion nodes containing `tfunctor` arguments in `instance_tfunctor`, resolving an uncaught exception from [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845))
- Updated Changes file to assign CVE numbers to the `intern.c` robustness fix (CVE-2026-28364, OSEC-2026-01 by Xavier Leroy and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)) and fill in reviewer credits for the relocatable compiler feature ([ocaml/ocaml#13728](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13728), [ocaml/ocaml#14014](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14014), [ocaml/ocaml#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14243))
- Added `AR` and `ARFLAGS` export in `Makefile.config`, deprecating `ARCMD` to improve build system integration ([Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), review by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), [ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- Corrected stdlib documentation: `Option.is_some` self-referential typo ([ocaml/ocaml#14855](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14855)) and `List.find_opt` clarification ([ocaml/ocaml#14851](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14851)) by [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875)
- Corrected `Patterns.deconstruct` documentation to reflect the function is total and does not raise exceptions ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [ocaml/ocaml#14859](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14859))
group_summaries.activity_summary: New PRs opened for configurable domain stack size, Dynarray sorting, Windows cross-compilation fixes, and memory model documentation.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened cross-compilation fixes for Windows addressing dummy file extensions in `installcross`, CC variable quoting for Flexlink, and preventing clang-cl from synthesizing `wcslen` calls via `-fno-builtin-wcslen` ([ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850))
- [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) opened a PR implementing configurable domain stack size via a new `S=` parameter in `OCAMLRUNPARAM`, addressing a feature request for C FFI-heavy workloads ([ocaml/ocaml#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860), related to [ocaml/ocaml#14195](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14195))
- [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) also opened a PR implementing `Dynarray.sort`, `Dynarray.stable_sort`, and `Dynarray.stable_sort_sub` by delegating to `Array.stable_sort_sub` on the backing array ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a PR adding memory model documentation comments in `memory.c` explaining release fences and "publication safety" for initialising writes, drawing on [ocaml/ocaml#10995](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10995) ([ocaml/ocaml#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857))
- [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues work on native `Iatomic_fetch_add` across amd64, arm64, RISC-V, and POWER, with review from [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) on release fence correctness ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) continues seeking feedback on `Weak.{iter, iteri, foldi_left}` additions, with `foldi_left` implemented as a C primitive to avoid per-element `option` wrapping allocations ([ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792))
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) maintains an open draft PR for extensible IO channels implementing the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md), under stdlib team discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775))
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) has two open PRs: `-rectypes` testing in expect tests ([ocaml/ocaml#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)) and allowing aliases in signature inclusion checks ([ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746))
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open PR adding weak pointer and ephemeron repro test cases for multicoretests failures from [ocaml/ocaml#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349), noting tests now pass on trunk ([ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745))
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged a fix for the GHA cygwin-packages download directory causing CI cache misses (~20 seconds per run saved) ([ocaml/ocaml#14852](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14852))
- Long-standing soundness bug with polymorphism and row types ([ocaml/ocaml#10840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10840)) was closed by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), noting the reported crash examples are now rejected by the typechecker
group_summaries.cross_repository_work: - [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)'s draft PR for extensible IO channels ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)) implements the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) from the [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) repository, making it a direct dependency between the two group repositories
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Yotam Barnoy](https://github.com/bluddy) proposed lighter syntax for deep record updates in [ocaml/ocaml#14854](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14854), suggesting `{bar with {foo with x=2}}` as an alternative to `{bar with foo={bar.foo with x=2}}`, with comparisons to the dotted-path syntax from [ocaml/ocaml#290](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/290)
- Two newcomers opened contribution guidance discussions: [@gperdutoutmoncodetipe](https://github.com/gperdutoutmoncodetipe) in [ocaml/ocaml#13901](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13901) and [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) in [ocaml/ocaml#14849](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14849); [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) subsequently contributed two documentation fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14851](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14851), [ocaml/ocaml#14855](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14855)) that were merged the same week
- [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14861](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14861) reporting that object finalizers declared in `initializer` can prevent GC collection
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14858](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14858) on the broken `make world && make install` workflow
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Windows and cross-compilation work received sustained attention: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) landed a security fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14853](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14853)), [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged a CI cache fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14852](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14852)) and opened further `installcross` fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850))
- Security hardening received formal CVE assignments for two previously merged fixes: CVE-2026-28364 / OSEC-2026-01 for `intern.c` robustness and CVE-2026-41083 / OSEC-2026-05 for `Filename.quote_command` Windows injection
- Newcomer contributions translated into merged work: [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) went from a newcomer Q&A discussion to having two documentation PRs merged within the same week ([ocaml/ocaml#14851](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14851), [ocaml/ocaml#14855](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14855)), and [@Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) submitted PRs for both a domain stack size feature ([ocaml/ocaml#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860)) and `Dynarray.sort` ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
- Memory model documentation is being actively improved: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)'s open PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857)) and the ongoing `Iatomic_fetch_add` review ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) both reflect careful attention to documenting and correctly implementing the OCaml multicore memory model guarantees

group_summaries.brief_summary: eio gains incremental directory reading and subtree rename; ocaml-uring adds socket CLOEXEC default and new I/O bindings.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: eio adds Path.with_dir_entries/read_dir_entries and Path.open_subtree; ocaml-uring defaults sockets to CLOEXEC.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`Path.open_subtree`** and **`Path.with_subtree`** replace `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` in [ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853) with names that communicate capability-confinement semantics, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)
- **`Path.with_dir_entries`** provides incremental directory entry reading as a lazy `Seq.t` of `(File.Stat.kind * string)` pairs, avoiding sort overhead and showing 10–13× speedups on Linux for recursive walks ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Path.read_dir_entries`** returns a sorted `(File.Stat.kind * string) list` including file type information, eliminating the need for a separate `kind` call per entry ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Eio_linux.Low_level.read_some_dir`** exposes batched directory entry reading with kind from the Linux backend ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`Eio_posix.Low_level.with_dir_entries`** provides the POSIX-backend counterpart with batched reads and worker-thread directory opening ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- **`eio_linux` ENOMEM fallback**: when `io_uring_queue_init` fails with ENOMEM on memory-constrained systems, `eio_linux` now falls back to `eio_posix` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#852](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/852))
- **Socket CLOEXEC default** in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151): `socket` now uses `Sock_flags.cloexec` as the default `?flags` value, preventing fd leaks into child processes; merged by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)
- **Per-operation RWF flags** proposed in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154): exposes `preadv2`/`pwritev2` flags (`RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_NOCACHE`) for use by `eio_linux` on recent 6.x kernels
- **Vectored fixed-buffer bindings** proposed in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155): corrects misnamed C stubs and adds proper `readv`/`writev` for fixed-buffer operations
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring); the remaining seven repositories (domainslib, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no recorded commits or PRs
- In eio, five PRs merged: incremental directory reading ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)), `Path.open_subtree` rename ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)), ENOMEM fallback ([ocaml-multicore/eio#852](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/852)), scheduler simplification ([ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)), and removal of unused retry logic ([ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851))
- In ocaml-uring, one PR merged ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151)), two new PRs opened ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155)), one draft PR active ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)), and two older PRs closed ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101))
- Three contributors were active: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)
group_summaries.activity_summary: eio scheduler simplified by moving retry logic to fibers; ocaml-uring bytes migration benchmarked and three open PRs await review.
group_summaries.activity:
- **eio scheduler simplification** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)): read/write retries moved out of the scheduler into calling fibers, removing the `Read`/`Write` job types, `file_offset` type, and `amount` type; merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)
- **Removal of unused fixed-buffer retry logic** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851)): EAGAIN/EINTR retries that cannot trigger since switching to `DEFER_TASKRUN` removed; merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)
- **`add ftruncate/fdatasync and use uring for fsync/shutdown/socket`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)): opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), adds `ftruncate`/`fdatasync` to the Linux backend and wires newer io_uring operations for `fsync`, `shutdown`, and socket calls; awaiting review
- **`Eio_unix.Sockopt` module** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575)): rebased by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) this week; adds a GADT-based socket options interface and is seeking design feedback on `SOCKET` module type integration
- **`chmod` support in `Eio_linux` and `Eio_posix`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)): originally by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), rebased by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) with uring `nofollow` flag usage and `File.Unix_perm.t` alignment; awaiting final review
- **Long-standing deadlock issue closed** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#700](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/700)): "Occasional deadlocks with systhreads" resolved this week
- **`bytes` buffer migration draft** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) shared microbenchmarks showing improved large-buffer allocation and I/O throughput but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor; ongoing
- **Experimental Cstruct-to-Bstruct PR closed** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101)): closed by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) in favour of the broader bytes migration in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **ocaml-uring socket CLOEXEC → eio**: merging [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) (socket CLOEXEC default) unblocks [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), which wires up uring socket creation directly in `eio_linux`
- **ocaml-uring RWF flags → eio_linux**: the new [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#154](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/154) (per-operation RWF flags) is explicitly targeted at `eio_linux` use, enabling `RWF_DSYNC` to replace separate `fsync` calls and `RWF_NOCACHE` for large copies
- **ocaml-uring errno closure referenced eio PRs**: discussion closing [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152) explicitly referenced [ocaml-multicore/eio#788](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/788) and [ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851) as context for why a separate errno module adds limited value when `Unix_error` is always required by Eio's `Low_level` fallback paths
- **eio filesystem API expansion feeds ocaml-uring vectored I/O**: the ftruncate/fdatasync PR ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)) and vectored fixed-buffer PR ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#155](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/155)) are part of the same initiative to extend low-level I/O coverage across both layers
- **mirage/ocaml-tar cross-project reference**: [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) cited [mirage/ocaml-tar#177](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-tar/issues/177) as a real-world case where the old `open_dir` name caused a security confusion, directly motivating the `Path.open_subtree` rename in [ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **`Path.open_subtree` naming debate** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) questioned whether the confusion was a documentation problem; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued `with_open_dir` sounds like a directory-listing API rather than a capability-confinement primitive; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) cited [mirage/ocaml-tar#177](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-tar/issues/177) as a concrete real-world security confusion caused by the old name
- **Distinct types for confined vs. unconfined directory handles** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued static typing cannot track whether symlink traversal escapes a subtree; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) proposed renaming `Path.(/)` to `Path.extend_unconfined` as a use-site signal instead
- **Minimum kernel version proposal for `eio_linux`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) discussed requiring kernel 6.17+/7.x+ to eliminate many op-probe workarounds needed for newer io_uring features
- **`bytes` vs `bigarray` performance tradeoffs** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) asked for benchmark evidence; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reported improved large-buffer allocation (avoiding bigarray finalizer overhead) but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB GC-pinning minimum; conclusion was to coalesce small writes before submission
- **`caml_hash_variant` compile-time evaluation** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) tested in Compiler Explorer and found gcc/clang optimise the call away, but this does not work in OCaml C stubs since `caml_hash_variant` is defined in a `.c` file rather than a header
- **Linux errno module utility** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)): [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) concluded that since `Unix_error` is always needed for `Low_level` fallback paths in Eio, a Linux-specific errno module adds limited value; preference expressed for contributing useful errnos upstream to OCaml
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Filesystem API surface expansion**: `with_dir_entries`/`read_dir_entries` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) and pending PRs for `ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, `fsync`, `chmod`, and socket options ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850), [ocaml-multicore/eio#785](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/785)) continue a pattern of broadening eio's low-level filesystem and I/O coverage
- **Capability-confinement terminology tightening**: the `open_subtree`/`with_subtree` rename ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)) and discussion of `Path.extend_unconfined` suggest ongoing effort to surface security semantics at the API level rather than relying on documentation
- **io_uring scheduler internalization**: moving retry logic from the scheduler into fibers ([ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)) and removing dead retry paths ([ocaml-multicore/eio#851](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/851)) reflect a deliberate simplification of the `eio_linux` scheduler toward a leaner design
- **Migration from Cstruct/bigarray to `bytes`**: closure of [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101) in favour of [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) shows a deliberate effort to reduce C-backed memory dependencies and improve standard OCaml library interoperability
- **Safer-by-default API conventions**: the CLOEXEC change in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) reflects a pattern of making secure behavior opt-out rather than opt-in, consistent with modern Linux socket best practices
- **Minimum kernel version consideration**: discussion in [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) about requiring kernel 6.17+/7.x+ for `eio_linux` suggests willingness to drop older kernel support in exchange for significantly simpler backend code

weekly_summary.brief_summary: OCaml 5.4 merged into OxCaml; two CVEs fixed in core; OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 landed in opam-repository; eio gains incremental directory reading.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: CVE security fixes in core; OCaml 5.4 in OxCaml; eio Path.with_dir_entries; js_of_ocaml WASI runtime; odoc OxCaml modalities; 25+ opam packages.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **shell injection vulnerability** in `Filename.quote_command` on Windows (CVE-2026-41083, OSEC-2026-05) — filenames with special characters like `&` in redirection arguments were not properly quoted; implemented by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), reviewed by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14853](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14853))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **inverted `mark_header` lazy tag check** — a regression from [ocaml/ocaml#14816](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14816) causing corruption and segfaults in multi-domain programs using laziness ([ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **infinite loop in `runtime_events.c`** `write_to_ring` when an event exceeds ring buffer size; runtime now emits a fatal error with the minimum required `e=` value (Michael Bacarella, reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [ocaml/ocaml#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **Flambda infinite recursive stub inlining** miscompilation discovered by Nathanaëlle Courant and Vincent Laviron, fixed by Pierre Chambart ([ocaml/ocaml#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828), [ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **modular explicit type expansion** to correctly update expansion nodes containing `tfunctor` arguments, resolving an uncaught exception from [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Added **`AR` and `ARFLAGS` export in `Makefile.config`**, deprecating `ARCMD` to improve build system integration ([Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA), [ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) landed on June 12 in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), bringing in the full 5.4 branch including stdlib updates, runtime changes, and toolchain improvements with a magic number bump
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ Fixed **correct handling of concurrent lazy header marking** in the major GC, backported from [ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856): resolves potential SEGVs or heap corruption when two domains race on a lazy block ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6228))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Dune 3.22.2+ox package family** (16 sub-packages including `chrome-trace`, `dune-build-info`, `dune-rpc`, `stdune`, `xdg`) added to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) with `oxcaml-dune.patch` compatibility ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Path.with_dir_entries`** in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) provides incremental directory entry reading as a lazy `Seq.t` of `(File.Stat.kind * string)` pairs, with 10–13× speedups on Linux for recursive walks ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Path.read_dir_entries`** returns a sorted `(File.Stat.kind * string) list` with file type information, eliminating separate `kind` calls per entry ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Path.open_subtree`** and **`Path.with_subtree`** replace `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` with names communicating capability-confinement semantics, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Socket `CLOEXEC` default** in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring): `socket` now uses `Sock_flags.cloexec` as default, preventing fd leaks into child processes ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **WASI 0.1 runtime** added to `wasm_of_ocaml` via `--enable wasi` flag ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon); supports Wizard engine and wasmtime
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml modalities** rendered across all odoc output backends (HTML, LaTeX, man pages, markdown) via [ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Configurator `query_variable`** adds `Pkg_config.query_variable` to query arbitrary pkg-config variables per package in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#15078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15078)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Let-punning locate fix** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin): `locate` on punned bindings like `let x in` or `let+ x in` now jumps to the source binding correctly ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **13 js_of_ocaml runtime correctness fixes** merged including `Ephemeron.blit_key` data corruption ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2264](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2264)), `Int64.shift_right` sign extension ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2278](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2278)), BLOCK32 unsigned shift ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2275](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2275)), `Sys.rename`-to-self deletion ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2268](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2268)), and Str SIMPLEOPT infinite loop ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2277))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5.0~rc1** packaged in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) with `ocaml-compiler`, `ocaml-base-compiler`, and variants ([ocaml/opam-repository#30023](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30023))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **25+ new or updated packages** in opam-repository including **awskit** 0.1.0 (AWS infrastructure with Eio/Lwt adapters, [ocaml/opam-repository#29980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29980)), **migra** 1.0.0 (database migrations, [ocaml/opam-repository#30002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30002)), **cabal** 0.1.0 (agent execution primitives, [ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008)), **ocaml-ai-sdk** 0.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005)), **transom** 0.2.0 (WebView desktop apps, [ocaml/opam-repository#30037](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30037)), and **miou** 0.7.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Published **Backstage article on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI prototype** covering WASI preview1 backend, CPS effects compilation, and a call for production users or sponsors ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ Added **`CLAUDE.md` developer guide** to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) with AI-assisted development context covering build commands, architecture, EML templating, data flow, and CI/deployment ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))

-**13 js_of_ocaml runtime correctness fixes** merged including `Ephemeron.blit_key` data corruption ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2264](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2264)), `Int64.shift_right` sign extension ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2278](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2278)), BLOCK32 unsigned shift ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2275](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2275)), `Sys.rename`-to-self deletion ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2268](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2268)), and Str SIMPLEOPT infinite loop ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2277))
ecosystem: -**25+ new or updated packages** in opam-repository including **awskit** 0.1.0 (AWS infrastructure with Eio/Lwt adapters, [ocaml/opam-repository#29980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29980)), **migra** 1.0.0 (database migrations, [ocaml/opam-repository#30002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30002)), **cabal** 0.1.0 (agent execution primitives, [ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008)), **ocaml-ai-sdk** 0.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005)), **transom** 0.2.0 (WebView desktop apps, [ocaml/opam-repository#30037](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30037)), and **miou** 0.7.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#30009](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30009))

-Published **Backstage article on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI prototype** covering WASI preview1 backend, CPS effects compilation, and a call for production users or sponsors ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667))

-Added **`CLAUDE.md` developer guide** to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) with AI-assisted development context covering build commands, architecture, EML templating, data flow, and CI/deployment ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585))
oxcaml: -**OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) landed on June 12 in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), bringing in the full 5.4 branch including stdlib updates, runtime changes, and toolchain improvements with a magic number bump

-Fixed **correct handling of concurrent lazy header marking** in the major GC, backported from [ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856): resolves potential SEGVs or heap corruption when two domains race on a lazy block ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6228))

-**Dune 3.22.2+ox package family** (16 sub-packages including `chrome-trace`, `dune-build-info`, `dune-rpc`, `stdune`, `xdg`) added to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) with `oxcaml-dune.patch` compatibility ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49))
multicore: -**`Path.with_dir_entries`** in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) provides incremental directory entry reading as a lazy `Seq.t` of `(File.Stat.kind * string)` pairs, with 10–13× speedups on Linux for recursive walks ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))

-**`Path.read_dir_entries`** returns a sorted `(File.Stat.kind * string) list` with file type information, eliminating separate `kind` calls per entry ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821))

-**`Path.open_subtree`** and **`Path.with_subtree`** replace `open_dir`/`with_open_dir` with names communicating capability-confinement semantics, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853))

-**Socket `CLOEXEC` default** in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring): `socket` now uses `Sock_flags.cloexec` as default, preventing fd leaks into child processes ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) had two CVE security fixes land (CVE-2026-41083 for `Filename.quote_command` Windows injection and CVE-2026-28364 for `intern.c` robustness), a GC `mark_header` regression fix, a Flambda miscompilation correction, and new PRs for configurable domain stack size ([ocaml/ocaml#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14860)) and `Dynarray.sort` ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848)); newcomer [adith nk](https://github.com/adithn875) went from a first contribution question to having two documentation fixes merged in the same week
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) completed the **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325)) on June 12 after months of work, triggering 40+ new PRs for post-merge cleanup including unboxed types, mode error improvements, name mangling test infrastructure, and Merlin synchronisation; [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) added 16 Dune 3.22.2+ox packages and merged constraint fixes
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) merged 5 PRs including the incremental directory reading API ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) and the `Path.open_subtree` capability rename ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)); [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) merged CLOEXEC defaulting ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151)) and saw 2 new PRs for per-operation RWF flags and vectored fixed-buffer bindings
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) saw the highest volume with the WASI runtime landing ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), docs migrated to odoc ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)), 13 merged runtime correctness fixes, and 15+ newly opened follow-up PRs from a systematic review; [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) advanced RPC architecture refactoring and Memo API expansion; [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) opened a draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) saw OCaml 5.5.0~rc1 land alongside 25+ new or updated packages spanning AWS SDKs, database tooling, unikernel networking (Miou/robur-coop coordinated releases), AI/agent libraries, and static analysis; [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) added a `CLAUDE.md` AI dev guide, contrast fixes for OCaml Workshop 2026 date pills, and a backstage post on `wasm_of_ocaml` WASI
