weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened cross-compilation fixes for Windows (`installcross` dummy file extensions, CC variable quoting for Flexlink, `-fno-builtin-wcslen`) ([ocaml/ocaml#14850](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14850)) and merged a CI cache fix saving ~20 seconds per run ([ocaml/ocaml#14852](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14852))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) opened a PR adding **memory model documentation comments** in `memory.c` explaining release fences and "publication safety" for initialising writes ([ocaml/ocaml#14857](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14857))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues native `Iatomic_fetch_add` work across amd64, arm64, RISC-V, and POWER backends with review from [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) maintains an open draft PR for extensible IO channels implementing the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) under stdlib team discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode error reporting improvements** from [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) add annotation-hint source locations and rework subsumption error printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6249](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6249), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6227](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6227)); a fix to `Const.comonadic_to_monadic_min` correcting incorrect staticity errors in loops also landed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6244](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6244))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Unboxed types representation work** from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): requiring `[@all_void_constructor]` on void-argument constructors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6256](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6256)), fixing inline record representation for `any` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6240](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6240)), and restricting variant manipulation to types with determined layouts ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6238](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6238))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Name mangling test infrastructure** from [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist): split [oxcaml/oxcaml#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552) into four targeted PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6234](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6234), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6235), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6236](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6236), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6237)); [@shym](https://github.com/shym) added structured mangling improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6242))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a draft introducing `caml_add_extent` for handing external memory regions to the GC ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)) and a PR to retire runtime 4 across 2,000+ files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin sync** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a CI enforcement script ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)) and 5.4 Merlin upgrade PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)); the large draft merge PR in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) was closed after conflicts across 896 files
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **eio scheduler simplification** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)): read/write retries moved from the scheduler into calling fibers, removing `Read`/`Write` job types; merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, uring-based `fsync`/`shutdown`/`socket`** PR opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)); awaiting review
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Eio_unix.Sockopt` module** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575)) rebased by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), adding GADT-based socket options and seeking design feedback
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`bytes` buffer migration draft** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)) shows improved large-buffer allocation performance but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor; experimental Cstruct-to-Bstruct PR closed in its favour ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune RPC server refactoring** ([ocaml/dune#15211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15211), [ocaml/dune#15202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15202), [ocaml/dune#15209](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15209)): session object replaced with a record, `Rpc.Global` indirection removed, server creation moved to socket creation time by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune `Build_loop` API redesign** ([ocaml/dune#15191](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15191)): `submit_rpc_request`, `cancel_rpc_requests_by_session`, and `sticky_goal` parameter introduced for concurrent RPC builds by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam `init --bare` performance fix** ([ocaml/opam#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957)): prevents unnecessary compiler selection when no compiler is needed, fixing [ocaml/opam#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815) by [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam API dead-code removal** ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)): 366 unused exported values, fields, and constructors removed by [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **merlin draft OCaml 5.5 upgrade** ([ocaml/merlin#2077](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2077)): 462-file draft PR opened by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) to keep Merlin in sync with upstream compiler
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml newly opened runtime fix PRs** from systematic review ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270)): exact float formatting ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2280](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2280)), float array hashing ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2283](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2283)), filesystem fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2286](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2286)), Unix fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2287](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2287)), and Domain.spawn exception handling ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2291](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2291))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Windows CI hardened** in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998)) and `conf-*` packages updated for 0install solver compatibility on Windows ([ocaml/opam-repository#30029](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30029))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **dune 3.24 compatibility**: `crunch` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30035](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30035)), `lintcstubs` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30032](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30032)), and `merlin` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30044](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30044)) all required conditional test gating on `dune < 3.24` due to install layout changes

-**Name mangling test infrastructure** from [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist): split [oxcaml/oxcaml#5552](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5552) into four targeted PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6234](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6234), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6235](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6235), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6236](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6236), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6237](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6237)); [@shym](https://github.com/shym) added structured mangling improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6242))

-[Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opened a draft introducing `caml_add_extent` for handing external memory regions to the GC ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6248](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6248)) and a PR to retire runtime 4 across 2,000+ files ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6218](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6218))

-**Merlin sync** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a CI enforcement script ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)) and 5.4 Merlin upgrade PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)); the large draft merge PR in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) was closed after conflicts across 896 files
multicore: -**eio scheduler simplification** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)): read/write retries moved from the scheduler into calling fibers, removing `Read`/`Write` job types; merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)

-**`ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, uring-based `fsync`/`shutdown`/`socket`** PR opened by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)); awaiting review

-**`Eio_unix.Sockopt` module** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575)) rebased by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), adding GADT-based socket options and seeking design feedback

-**`bytes` buffer migration draft** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)) shows improved large-buffer allocation performance but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor; experimental Cstruct-to-Bstruct PR closed in its favour ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#101](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/101))

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin synchronisation across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a CI enforcement script (`assert-in-sync.sh`) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6245](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6245)) while the large draft merge PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) was closed after conflicts across 896 files; the 5.4 Merlin upgrade PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6250](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6250)) now coordinates this effort
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune compatibility chain from [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)**: Dune's shift to pure OCaml patch implementation ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)) broke OxCaml patch files; [oxcaml/opam-repository#51](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/51) awaits resolution via [ocaml/dune#15146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15146), with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) bridging both discussions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **dune 3.24 install layout affects merlin**: dune install layout changes ([ocaml/dune#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432)) broke merlin's no-dune test; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) diagnosed and fixed it in [ocaml/merlin#2078](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2078), identified via opam reverse-dependency tests in [ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam `.install` prefix sections blocked by opam review, awaited by dune**: [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)'s [ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) (adding `prefix`/`prefixexec` sections) is queued behind [ocaml/opam#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956); Ward explicitly asked about release timeline for dune to begin depending on the feature

- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **ocaml-uring socket CLOEXEC unblocks eio**: merging [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) (socket CLOEXEC default) unblocks [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), which wires up uring socket creation directly in `eio_linux`; [mirage/ocaml-tar#177](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-tar/issues/177) was cited as a real-world security confusion motivating the `Path.open_subtree` rename in [ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)
- __RUMINANT:core__ __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **GC fix ported cross-fork**: the `mark_header` regression fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14856](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14856)) was simultaneously backported to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) as [oxcaml/oxcaml#6228](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6228), continuing the pattern of safety fixes flowing both directions between the main and OxCaml repos
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **[David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) active across dune and opam**: fixed pkgconf `--personality` in dune ([ocaml/dune#15142](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15142)) and opened an `opam lint` warning-demotion request in opam ([ocaml/opam#6964](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6964)), motivated by [ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29998)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **AI and agent tooling across repos**: opam-repository added `ocaml-ai-sdk.0.4` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30005](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30005)) and `cabal.0.1.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#30008](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30008)) targeting AI/LLM use cases, while ocaml.org added a `CLAUDE.md` guide ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3585](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3585)) formalising AI-assisted development

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml systematic runtime review** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) filed comprehensive reviews of all `runtime/js/*.js` and `runtime/wasm/*.wat` files using differential testing (BigInt oracles with 200k/20k cases, RFC test vectors for MD5/BLAKE2, 300-case zstd differential, fuzzed mlBytes/UTF-8 codec tests), cataloguing 30+ bugs across correctness, crashes, and JS/Wasm parity
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **js_of_ocaml compiler output soundness issues** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2285](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2285)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) identified unsound optimisations in `js_simpl.ml` and `js_traverse.ml` — if-fusion collapsing effectful expressions and constant-if folding dropping `var` declarations, causing `ReferenceError` in strict/ESM contexts
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocamlgrep structural search proposal** ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating typed-AST structural search into ocaml-lsp; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) argued editor plugins are a better fit than the LSP server; work supervised by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) at LexiFi
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam solver downgrade behavior** ([ocaml/opam discussions #6966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/discussions/6966)): [lexa](https://github.com/qexat) questioned why `opam upgrade` proposed downgrading `ansifmt` from 2.0.0 to 0.3.0 to upgrade `re` to 1.14.0, raising questions about whether the default solver handles semantic versioning expectations appropriately
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune absolute path audit** ([ocaml/dune#12230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12230)): [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) opened sub-issues tracking commands that crash or misbehave on absolute paths (`dune utop`, `dune ocaml top`, `dune show targets`, `dune fmt` over RPC), each with a companion test PR
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Path.open_subtree` naming debate** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#853](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/853)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) questioned whether the confusion was a documentation problem; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued `with_open_dir` sounds like a directory-listing API rather than a capability-confinement primitive; [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) cited [mirage/ocaml-tar#177](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-tar/issues/177) as a concrete real-world security confusion caused by the old name
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Minimum kernel version proposal for `eio_linux`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) discussed requiring kernel 6.17+/7.x+ to eliminate many op-probe workarounds needed for newer io_uring features
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **AVX-512 support proposal** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6257](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6257)): [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) opened a detailed proposal covering EVEX encoding, ZMM register classes, opmask (k0–k7) additions, and a phased landing plan, requesting design coordination before implementation
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Unboxed float register clobber on domain resize** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6255](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6255)): [Ty Overby](https://github.com/TyOverby) reported that resizing a domain while an unboxed float is in a register clobbers the value, with a minimal reproducer using the `parallel` library
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Deep record update syntax** proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14854](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14854)): [Yotam Barnoy](https://github.com/bluddy) suggested `{bar with {foo with x=2}}` as an alternative to `{bar with foo={bar.foo with x=2}}`, with comparisons to the dotted-path syntax from [ocaml/ocaml#290](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/290)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Object finalizer GC prevention** ([ocaml/ocaml#14861](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14861)): [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots) reported that object finalizers declared in `initializer` can prevent GC collection
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **`ocaml-r.0.7.0` macOS failures** ([ocaml/opam-repository#30007](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/30007)): [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) flagged failures due to `conf-r-mathlib` being too old; recommendation to merge anyway and update the conf package separately

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Post-5.4 merge consolidation**: completion of [oxcaml/oxcaml#5325](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5325) marks a shift from merge logistics to post-merge cleanup, test promotion, and leveraging 5.4 features such as the Compression module ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5533](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5533)) and updated runtime header hygiene ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6138](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6138))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Security hardening formalised**: CVE-2026-28364 (intern.c) and CVE-2026-41083 (Filename.quote_command Windows injection) both received formal OSEC CVE assignments this week, reflecting a maturing security disclosure process for the OCaml compiler
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Systematic AI-assisted correctness reviews in js_of_ocaml**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2263](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2263) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2270](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2270) use a review-plus-differential-testing methodology (BigInt oracles, RFC vectors, fuzz runs), driving clusters of 15+ PRs rather than individual one-off fixes — a repeatable pattern for runtime correctness improvement
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **JS/Wasm runtime convergence in js_of_ocaml**: many bug fixes explicitly cross-reference divergences between `runtime/js/*.js` and `runtime/wasm/*.wat`, with the Wasm cleanup PR [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2262](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2262) aiming to enable the same systematic review methodology for Wasm files
